My soul having been wounded by the bite of the serpent, hath been cleansed of its wounds by Thee O Christ, Who hath revealed Thy light to me, who of old lay in darkness and corruption; for through the Cross Thou didst descend into Hades and hast raised me up together with Thyself.
ode3.theotokion: By the supplications of Thy Mother who knew not a man, bestow peace upon the world, O Savior; and grant victory over their adversaries to all Orthodox Christians, deeming them who glorify Thee, worthy of Thine ineffable glory.

ode6.irmos: Sailing in the tempest of the cares of life, together with the ship I have been submerged by sins, and cast to the soul-corrupting beast, wherefore like Jonah I cry to Thee, O Christ: Lead me up from the deadly abyss.
troparia: The souls of the righteous, who were held in bondage and forsaken in Hades, remembered Thee and prayed for salvation from Thee, which Thou didst grant unto them through Thy Cross, O Christ, when in Thy compassion Thou didst descend into the nether regions of the earth.

The choir of Apostles despaired of ever gazing again upon Thy living temple not made with hands, which had been destroyed by the passion, but praying beyond hope they were granted to proclaim everywhere that Thou art Risen.
ode6.theotokion: Who can explain the manner of thine ineffable child-bearing for our sake, O most immaculate Virgin, Bride of God? For God the Word, who is uncircumscribable, uniting with thee, became flesh from thee.

afterKathisma2.text: When Life was laying in the tomb and a seal laid upon the stone, the soldiers guarded Christ as a sleeping King and the Angels glorified Him as immortal God; while the women cried aloud: “The Lord is risen, granting the world great mercy.”

By Thy burial for three days Thou didst despoil death, and by Thy lifebearing Arising Thou didst raise corrupted mankind, O Christ Lord, as the
Lover of mankind. Glory be to Thee!

afterKathisma2.label: Glory…, Now…, Theotokion
afterKathisma2.text: O Virgin Theotokos unceasingly entreat Christ our God, who wast crucified for us and arose again destroying the dominion of death, that He save our souls.

afterKathisma3.text: While the grave was sealed, O Christ God, Thou, the Life, didst shine forth from the tomb; and while the doors were shut, Thou, the Resurrection of all, didst appear unto Thy Disciples, and through them renewed a right Spirit within us, according to Thy great mercy.

Bringing sweet spices with their tears, the women ran to the tomb, and while the soldiers guarded Thee, the King of all, they spake one to another: “Who will roll away the stone for us? The Angel of great Counsel hath risen, trampling down death.” O All-powerful Lord, glory be to Thee!

afterKathisma3.label: Glory…, Now…, Theotokion
afterKathisma3.text: Rejoice Virgin Theotokos, full of grace, haven and protection of the race of mankind, for from Thee the Redeemer of the world hath taken flesh, for thou alone art a Mother and Virgin, ever blessed and exceedingly glorified; intercede with Christ God to grant peace unto all the world.

matins.hypakoe: O Christ God, who took upon Thyself our form and endured the Cross in the body, save me by Thy Resurrection, as Thou alone lovest mankind.

troparia:
Having turned back the captivity of Zion from error, quicken me also O
Savior, and deliver me from slavery to the passions.
He who soweth tribulations in the south with tears of fasting, will reap with joy the sheaves of nourishment of eternal life.
In the Holy Spirit is the source of divine treasures, from Him cometh wisdom, intelligence, and fear; and to Him belongeth praise, glory, honor and worship.

Unless the Lord buildeth the house of the soul, in vain do we labor; for without Him no deed or word can be perfected.
Of the fruit of the womb the Saints, moved by the Spirit, sprout forth the
Father’s teachings of filial adoption.

By the Holy Spirit all things have their being; in the presence of all He is
God, the sovereign of the universe, Light unapproachable, Life of all.

Those who fear the Lord and find the ways of life, now and always are blessed with immortal glory.
As thou beholdest thine offspring like shoots around thy table, rejoice, and be glad, bringing them to Christ the Chief Shepherd of all.
In the Holy Spirit there be an abundance of grace, riches of glory and a great depth of judgments; for He is to be served as identical in glory and honor with the Father and the Son.

prokeimenon.refrain: Arise, O Lord my God, let Thy hand be lifted high; forget not Thy paupers to the end.
prokeimenon.verse: I will confess Thee, O Lord, with my whole heart; I will tell of all Thy wonders.

matins.postGospelSticheron: Jesus having risen from the grave, as He foretold, hath given us life eternal, and great mercy.

laudsStichera.verse: To do among them the judgment that is written This glory shall be to all His saints.
laudsStichera.text: Christ is risen from the dead, loosing the bonds of death; O earth! proclaim the good tidings of great joy; and ye heavens praise the glory of God.

laudsStichera.verse: Praise ye God in His saints, praise Him in the firmament of His power.
laudsStichera.text: Having seen the Resurrection of Christ, let us worship the holy Lord Jesus, the only sinless one.

laudsStichera.verse: Praise Him for His mighty acts, praise Him according to the multitude of His greatness.
laudsStichera.text:
We cease not to worship the Holy Resurrection of Christ, for He hath saved us from our iniquities, Holy is the Lord Jesus who hath shown us the
Resurrection.

laudsStichera.verse: Praise Him with the sound of trumpet, praise Him with the psaltery and harp.
laudsStichera.text: What shall we render unto the Lord for all that He hath rendered unto us? For our sake God dwelt among us; for our corrupted nature the Word became flesh and dwelt within us; unto the ungrateful He is the Benefactor; unto prisoners He is the Liberator; unto those in darkness He is the Sun of justice; the path unto the Cross; the Light unto Hades; Life unto death; Resurrection for the fallen: to Him we cry aloud: “Our God, glory be to Thee!”

laudsStichera.verse: Praise Him with timbrel and dance, praise him with strings and flute.
laudsStichera.text: By Thy mighty power, O Lord, Thou hast destroyed the gates of Hades and abolished the dominion of death; raising with Thyself the dead who slept therein from eternity in darkness, by Thy divine and glorious Resurrection, as King of the universe and as God All-powerful.

laudsStichera.verse: Praise Him with tuneful cymbals, praise Him with cymbals of jubilation. Let every breath praise the Lord.
laudsStichera.text: Come, let us rejoice in the Lord and be glad in His Resurrection; for He hath raised the dead with Himself from the indestructible bonds of Hades, and as God hath bestowed upon the world eternal life and great mercy.

laudsStichera.verse: Arise, O Lord my God, let Thy hands be lifted high; forget not Thy paupers to the end.
laudsStichera.text:
A radiant Angel sat on the stone of the grave that held Life, and announced the good tidings to the Myrrh-bearing women saying: “The Lord is risen, as He foretold to you; announce to His Disciples that He goeth before you into
Galilee; while to the world He granteth eternal life and great mercy.”

laudsStichera.verse: I will confess Thee, O Lord, with my whole heart, I will tell of all Thy wonders.
laudsStichera.text:
O ye exceedingly wicked Jews, why did you reject the Cornerstone? This is the stone which God hath placed in Zion, God Who in the wilderness made water spring forth from the rock, and for us poured forth immortality from His side; this is the stone which was hewn from the Virginal mountain, by the will of the Son of man, who cometh again on the clouds of heaven before the
Ancient of days, as Daniel hath said, and His Kingdom is everlasting.

canonIrmoi.1: At thy command O Lord, the nature of the waters that beforehand flowed freely was transformed and became like the earth; whereby Israel having traversed them dryshod chanted unto Thee a hymn of victory.
canonIrmoi.3:
O Lord and Savior, Who in the beginning established the heavens by Thine all-powerful Word , and by the divine and all-accomplishing
Spirit hath granted them all their strength, do Thou establish me on the unshakeable rock of Thy confession.
canonIrmoi.4: Having never left the bosom of the Father, Thou didst descend to earth O Christ God, I have heard of the mystery of Thy dispensation, and I have glorified Thee, O only Lover of mankind.
canonIrmoi.5: Night is bereft of light for those without faith, О Christ, but for the faithful there is enlightenment in the sweetness of Thy words; wherefore, I rise early unto Thee and hymn Thy Divinity.
canonIrmoi.6: Sailing in the tempest of the cares of life, together with the ship I have been submerged by sins, and cast to the soul-corrupting beast, wherefore like Jonah I cry to Thee, O Christ: Lead me up from the deadly abyss.
canonIrmoi.7: Of old the Children were shown to be bedewed in the fiery furnace, chanting and praising the one God saying, 'supremely exalted and exceedingly glorified is the God of our Fathers'.
canonIrmoi.8:
Unconsumed by fire, the bush on Sinai spake unto Moses, slow of speech and stammering, and revealed God unto him; and zeal for God showed forth the three children who chanted hymns to be unvanquished by the fire. O all ye His works, praise ye the Lord and supremely exalt
Him throughout all ages.
canonIrmoi.9: Conceiving without knowing corruption, and lending thy flesh to the Word, O Mother unwedded and Virgin Theotokos, thou art the vessel of the Uncircumscribable One, and dwelling place of thy Creator, thee do we magnify.

1.refrain: Glory to Thy holy Resurrection, O Lord.
1.text: The tyranny of death was judged through a tree, O Lord, when Thou wast condemned to an unjust death; and so the prince of darkness having no power over Thee was rightfully cast out.

1.refrain: Glory to Thy holy Resurrection, O Lord.
1.text: Hades drew nigh to Thee and in vain strove to crush Thy body with its teeth, breaking its jaws upon Thee; wherefore, O Savior, putting aside the pangs of death, Thou didst arise on the third day.

1.refrain: Glory to Thy holy Resurrection, O Lord.
1.text: The sorrows of the foremother Eve have been done away with, for having escaped those sorrows, thou hast given birth without wedlock; and so knowing thee to be truly the Theotokos we all glorify thee.

1.refrain: Glory to Thy precious Cross and Resurrection, O Lord.
1.text: From His pierced side on the Cross the Savior hath poured forth two lifebearing fountains from Himself unto us. Let us sing unto Him, for He hath been glorified.

1.refrain: Glory to Thy precious Cross and Resurrection, O Lord.
1.text: By dwelling in a tomb and arising on the third day Christ hath granted mortals a pledge of incorruption. Let us hymn Him, for He hath been glorified.

1.refrain: Glory to Thy precious Cross and Resurrection, O Lord.
1.text: Alone thou wast revealed a virgin even after child-birth; for thou hast given birth to the Creator of the world in the flesh. Wherefore we all cry to thee, “Rejoice!”

1.refrain: Most holy Theotokos, save us.
1.text: Thou who hast brought forth the depths of divine compassion, O Virgin, illumine my soul with thine effulgent radiance, that I may worthily hymn the depths of thy wonders.

1.refrain: Most holy Theotokos, save us.
1.text: Seeing that we have been wounded by the arrow of sin, the Word, our benefactor, hath taken pity on us. Wherefore the most divine One, ineffably united Himself to flesh from thee, O all-pure one.

1.refrain: Most holy Theotokos, save us.
1.text:
The corrupt and mortal nature of mankind hath become subject to death, O
Sovereign Lady. But having conceived life, O most pure one, thou hast led it back from corruption to life.

3.refrain: Glory to Thy holy Resurrection, O Lord.
3.text: Ascending the Tree, O compassionate Savior, and willingly suffering pangs for our sake, Thou didst endure Thy wounds, O cause of peace and through them bring the faithful to salvation, O Merciful One, whereby we all have been reconciled to Thy Begetter.

3.refrain: Glory to Thy holy Resurrection, O Lord.
3.text:
My soul having been wounded by the bite of the serpent, hath been cleansed of its wounds by Thee O Christ, Who hath revealed Thy light to me, who of old lay in darkness and corruption; for through the Cross Thou didst descend into
Hades and hast raised me up together with Thyself.

3.refrain: Glory to Thy holy Resurrection, O Lord.
3.text:
By the supplications of Thy Mother who knew not a man, bestow peace upon the world, O Savior; and grant victory over their adversaries to all
Orthodox Christians, deeming them who glorify Thee, worthy of Thine ineffable glory.

3.refrain: Glory to Thy precious Cross and Resurrection, O Lord.
3.text:
Thou didst endure sufferings on the Cross and thus opened Paradise to the
Thief, as benefactor and God; establish my mind to do Thy will, O only Lover of mankind.

3.refrain: Glory to Thy precious Cross and Resurrection, O Lord.
3.text:
Thou didst arise from the tomb on the third day making life dawn forth upon all the world, as the giver of life and as God; do Thou establish my mind to do
Thy will, O only Lover of mankind.

3.refrain: Glory to Thy precious Cross and Resurrection, O Lord.
3.text: O Virgin-mother Mary, having conceived God without seed and delivered Eve from the curse, entreat God who became incarnate from Thee, to save thy flock.

3.refrain: Most holy Theotokos, save us.
3.text:
The serpent crawling out of Eden hath enticed me with the desire to be
Godlike and hurled me down to the earth, but He who is by nature compassionate and merciful, hath taken pity upon me, and having dwelt in thy womb, became like me O Virgin-Mother, making me divine.

3.refrain: Most holy Theotokos, save us.
3.text: Blessed is the fruit of thy womb, O Virgin Theotokos, the joy of all; for thou hast brought forth unto all the world the wellspring of joy and gladness, Who scattereth the sorrow of sin, O Bride of God.

3.refrain: Most holy Theotokos, save us.
3.text: O Virgin Birthgiver of God, thou hast given birth to Peace for our sake, calming the ancient enmity between mankind and God his Father, and through faith granting us to know grace.

4.refrain: Glory to Thy holy Resurrection, O Lord.
4.text: The innocent Master, incarnate of the Virgin, having delivered His own back to the scourges of a fallen slave, and enduring maltreatment, hath thereby done away with the charges laid against me.

4.refrain: Glory to Thy holy Resurrection, O Lord.
4.text: Standing before the judgment seat of lawless judges, He who as God fashioned mankind and justly judgeth the whole universe, is examined as a lawbreaker and struck by a hand of clay.

4.refrain: Glory to Thy holy Resurrection, O Lord.
4.text: As truly the Mother of God, implore thy Creator and Son to direct me, O all-immaculate one, to the saving haven of His glorious will.

4.refrain: Glory to Thy precious Cross and Resurrection, O Lord.
4.text: Not knowing sin, and becoming what Thou wast not, O Lord, by assuming the form of what was another’s, Thou hast saved the world, for by luring the tyrant (to slay Thee) Thou hast thereby slain him.

4.refrain: Glory to Thy precious Cross and Resurrection, O Lord.
4.text: Thou wast hung upon the Cross, O Lord, and, having done away with the sin of our forefather Adam, Thou hast filled our foremother Eve with joy; for Thou art come to save all of Thine anointed ones.

4.refrain: Glory to Thy precious Cross and Resurrection, O Lord.
4.text:
Born from a Virgin, Thou didst suffer death, but granted life to
Adam, who by His own will hath been led astray, for death trembled at thy strength, seeing Thee saving those who had suffered corruption.

4.refrain: Most holy Theotokos, save us.
4.text: Thou hast appeared before God to be above all creation, wholly chosen and fair, by the splendor of the light that doth pour forth from thee, wherefore we beseech thee to enlighten those who sing thy praises.

4.refrain: Most holy Theotokos, save us.
4.text: From thy pure blood, O Pure Virgin Mother, thou hast given birth to God in the flesh, Who hath redeemed us from our many sins, wherefore with love we glorify thee, hymning thy praises.

4.refrain: Most holy Theotokos, save us.
4.text: Rational nature, now initiated into the ineffable mystery of thy child-bearing, ministers as priest to the One who dawned from thee, O All-praised One.

5.refrain: Glory to Thy holy Resurrection, O Lord.
5.text: Thou art sold on behalf of Thy slaves, O Christ, and endurest blows, O cause of freedom for those who sing to Thee. I rise to Thee at dawn and sing the praises of Thy Divinity.

5.refrain: Glory to Thy holy Resurrection, O Lord.
5.text: By Thy divine power, O Christ, through the infirmity of the flesh Thou hast overpowered the strong one and through Thy Resurrection declared me a victor over death, O Savior.

5.refrain: Glory to Thy holy Resurrection, O Lord.
5.text: In a manner befitting God, O all-hymned Mother, thou hast given birth to God who become incarnate from thee, since thou hast not known union in wedlock, the conception was by the Holy Spirit.

5.refrain: Glory to Thy precious Cross and Resurrection, O Lord.
5.text: When Thou wast lifted upon Calvary, and numbered with the malefactors, the lights of the firmament hid themselves, the earth trembled, and the splendor of the Temple was rent in twain, thereby revealing the apostasy of the Jews.

5.refrain: Glory to Thy precious Cross and Resurrection, O Lord.
5.text: With hymns we glorify Thee Who hath destroyed the entire dominion of the tyrant through the incomprehensible strength of Thy Divinity and thus raised the dead by Thy Resurrection.

5.refrain: Glory to Thy precious Cross and Resurrection, O Lord.
5.text: O all-praised Theotokos, Mother of the King and God, by thine intercessions send down the pardon offences, to those who with faith and love ever glorify thee in hymns.

5.refrain: Most holy Theotokos, save us.
5.text: When Jacob saw the ladder reaching up to the heavens he fathomed an image of thy virginity; for through thee, O all-pure Sovereign Lady, God hath been united with mankind.

5.refrain: Most holy Theotokos, save us.
5.text: Having found eternal redemption through thee, O Virgin, we cry unto thee with fervor, “Rejoice, O Bride of God!” And rejoicing in thy light we praise thee in song, O All-hymned one.

5.refrain: Most holy Theotokos, save us.
5.text: The Bridegroom found thee, O Virgin, as an only lily among the thorns, effulgent with the brightness of purity and the light of virginity, O all-immaculate one, wherefore He hath made thee His Bride.

6.refrain: Glory to Thy holy Resurrection, O Lord.
6.text: The souls of the righteous, who were held in bondage and forsaken in Hades, remembered Thee and prayed for salvation from Thee, which Thou didst grant unto them through Thy Cross, O Christ, when in Thy compassion Thou didst descend into the nether regions of the earth.

6.refrain: Glory to Thy holy Resurrection, O Lord.
6.text: The choir of Apostles despaired of ever gazing again upon Thy living temple not made with hands, which had been destroyed by the passion, but praying beyond hope they were granted to proclaim everywhere that Thou art Risen.

6.refrain: Glory to Thy holy Resurrection, O Lord.
6.text: Who can explain the manner of thine ineffable child-bearing for our sake, O most immaculate Virgin, Bride of God? For God the Word, who is uncircumscribable, uniting with thee, became flesh from thee.

6.refrain: Glory to Thy precious Cross and Resurrection, O Lord.
6.text: Willingly lifted up upon the Cross, O Savior, Thou didst take prisoner the dominion of the enemy. For upon it, O loving Lord, Thou hast nailed the record of our sins.

6.refrain: Glory to Thy precious Cross and Resurrection, O Lord.
6.text:
Having risen from the dead with authority, O Savior, Thou hast raised up together with Thyself the race of mankind, granting us life and incorruption, O
Lover of mankind.

6.refrain: Glory to Thy precious Cross and Resurrection, O Lord.
6.text: O Theotokos, cease not to entreat our God, to whom thou hast ineffably given birth, that those who hymn thee, O pure ever-Virgin, may be delivered from all dangers.

6.refrain: Most holy Theotokos, save us.
6.text: Prototypes in the Law and the sayings of the Prophets clearly foretold that thou, O most pure Virgin, would give birth to the Benefactor of all creation, Who hath in many and varied ways bestowed wondrous benefits upon those who with faith hymn thee.

6.refrain: Most holy Theotokos, save us.
6.text: Adam, the first-formed, was of old exiled from the delights of Paradise by the wiles of the man-slayer, but thou, O Virgin who knew not wedlock, hast led him back again, by giving birth to Him who hath delivered us from transgression.

6.refrain: Most holy Theotokos, save us.
6.text: He who by His divine will and creative power hath brought the universe into being from nothing hath come forth from thy womb, O most pure Virgin, shining with the effulgence of divine lightning upon all those in the shadow of death.

7.refrain: Glory to Thy holy Resurrection, O Lord.
7.text: Adam having by his own will committed disobedience was slain by a tree, but by Christ’s obedience he was renewed. Because for my sake the most glorious Son of God hath been crucified.

7.refrain: Glory to Thy holy Resurrection, O Lord.
7.text: When Thou, O Christ, didst arise from the tomb, all creation sang Thy praises; for Thou hast blossomed forth with life; The resurrection of the dead unto those in Hades, and the most glorious light unto those lying in darkness.

7.refrain: Glory to Thy holy Resurrection, O Lord.
7.text:
Rejoice!, O daughter of Adam, who hath fallen into corruption!
Rejoice!, O only Bride of God! Rejoice!, for thou hast given birth to God the
Word and through Him banished corruption! Entreat Him, O most pure Virgin that we all may be saved.

7.refrain: Glory to Thy precious Cross and Resurrection, O Lord.
7.text:
On the Tree of the Cross Thou hast blunted the sting of sin, and by the lance in Thy side Thou hast abolished the record of Adam’s transgressions; Blessed art
Thou, O Lord, the God of our Fathers.

7.refrain: Glory to Thy precious Cross and Resurrection, O Lord.
7.text:
Thou wast pierced in Thy side and with the sprinkling of Thy divine Blood
Thou hast cleansed the earth which was polluted with the bloodshed of the folly of idolatry; Blessed art Thou, O Lord, the God of our Fathers.

7.refrain: Glory to Thy precious Cross and Resurrection, O Lord.
7.text:
O Birthgiver of God, thou hast dawned forth upon world the illumination that existed before the sun, Christ, Who hath delivered us from darkness and illumined with the knowledge of God, all who cry: “Blessed art thou,
O Lord, the God of our Fathers.”

7.refrain: Most holy Theotokos, save us.
7.text: Thou O Virgin, doth possess like an adornment of many colors wrought in gold, love for thy Creator and Lord; the supremely exalted and exceedingly glorified God of our Fathers.

7.refrain: Most holy Theotokos, save us.
7.text: When Isaiah of old received the coal and was cleansed, He saw in it the symbol of the birth of thine Offspring, O Maiden, the supremely exalted and exceedingly glorified God of our Fathers.

7.refrain: Most holy Theotokos, save us.
7.text: When the divine Prophets of old saw the symbols of thy divine child-bearing they raised their voices in harmony O Virgin, praising thee and crying: “O supremely exalted and exceedingly glorified God of our Fathers.”

8.refrain: Glory to Thy holy Resurrection, O Lord.
8.text: The most-pure spiritual Lamb, slaughtered for the sake of the world, brought to an end offerings made in accordance with the Law, and as God Who alone is without transgression, purified the world which ever crieth aloud, “All ye works of the Lord, praise ye the Lord, and supremely exalt Him throughout all ages.”

8.refrain: Glory to Thy holy Resurrection, O Lord.
8.text:
The Creator assumed our flesh, which was not incorruptible before the
Passion, but after His Passion and His Rising was rendered inaccessible to corruption, and thus reneweth mortals as they cry, “All ye works of the Lord, praise ye the Lord, and supremely exalt Him throughout all ages.”

8.refrain: Glory to Thy holy Resurrection, O Lord.
8.text:
Thy total purity and lack of blemish, O Virgin, hath purified the inhabited world from all filth and pollution, and thou hast become, O most pure one, the means by which we have been reconciled with God. Wherefore, O
Virgin, we bless and supremely exalt thee throughout all ages.

8.refrain: Glory to Thy precious Cross and Resurrection, O Lord.
8.text: Unto Him Who hath willingly endured the Passion and of His own will was nailed to a Cross, thereby abolishing the powers of Hades, hymn, O ye Priests, and supremely exalt Him, O ye peoples, throughout all ages.

8.refrain: Glory to Thy precious Cross and Resurrection, O Lord.
8.text: Unto Him Who hath abolished the dominion of death and arisen from the tomb in glory, saving mankind, hymn, O ye Priests, and supremely exalt Him, O ye peoples, throughout all ages.

8.refrain: Glory to Thy precious Cross and Resurrection, O Lord.
8.text:
Unto the Word, Who is alone compassionate and pre-eternal, and
Who became incarnate in the latter times from the Virgin, abolishing the ancient curse, hymn, O ye Priests, and supremely exalt Him, O ye peoples, throughout all ages.

8.refrain: Most holy Theotokos, save us.
8.text: By the splendor of thy child-bearing, O Birthgiver of God, thou hast wondrously enlightened all the inhabited world. For in thine arms thou didst carry the true God, Who hath rendered radiant the faithful who ever cry: “All ye works of the Lord, praise ye the Lord, and supremely exalt Him throughout all ages.”

8.refrain: Most holy Theotokos, save us.
8.text: O Pure Virgin, we devoutly sing the praises of thy womb, which ineffably contained our God in the flesh, Who hath bestowed the illumination of the knowledge of God upon all the faithful who ever cry: “All ye works of the Lord, praise ye the Lord, and supremely exalt Him throughout all ages.”

8.refrain: Most holy Theotokos, save us.
8.text: With the brilliance of thy light, O pure Theotokos, thou who hast borne the light, rendered resplendent those who sing thy praises; for thou hast appeared as a tabernacle of light, enlightening those who ever cry: “All ye works of the Lord, praise ye the Lord, and supremely exalt Him throughout all ages.”

9.refrain: Glory to Thy holy Resurrection, O Lord.
9.text: Cease your babblings, all ye who with errant minds put forward the notion that the Godhead endured the passion, for the Lord of glory, crucified in the flesh, could not be crucified in His divine nature, wherefore we magnify Him as one Hypostasis in two natures.

9.refrain: Glory to Thy holy Resurrection, O Lord.
9.text: All ye that do not believe the Resurrection of the flesh hasten now to the grave of Christ, and there learn that the flesh of the Giver of life was slain and arose again, in confirmation of the final Resurrection, in which we have placed our hope.

9.refrain: Glory to Thy holy Resurrection, O Lord.
9.text:
Trinitarian: As we honor not a Trinity of Deities but of Hypostases, not a
Unity of persons but of the Godhead, we cut off those who divide It, and confound those who dare to confuse the true understanding of the Trinity, Whom we magnify.

9.refrain: Glory to Thy precious Cross and Resurrection, O Lord.
9.text: Light from light, the radiance of the Father’s glory, illumining agelessly, Christ hath shone forth for the life of mortal mankind lying in gloom, dispelling the persecuting darkness, wherefore we the faithful unceasingly magnify Him.

9.refrain: Glory to Thy precious Cross and Resurrection, O Lord.
9.text: Let those who contemplate the sufferings of Christ in the flesh, and the strength of the Godhead in Christ, as accomplished in one compound nature, be confounded; for Christ died as a man, but rose again as the Creator of the universe.

9.refrain: Glory to Thy precious Cross and Resurrection, O Lord.
9.text: “Besides Thee I know no other God,” The Holy Church crieth out to Thee O Word, “Who chose me as Thine own bride from among the unbelieving nations.” by the prayers of her who gave birth to Thee grant salvation to the faithful, for thou art compassionate and lovest mankind.

9.refrain: Most holy Theotokos, save us.
9.text: Ever-virgin maiden, for our sake thou wast revealed to be the cause of eternal joy and gladness, having carried in thy womb the Redeemer, who delivereth those who in truth and by the inspiration of the divine Spirit honor Him as God.

9.refrain: Most holy Theotokos, save us.
9.text:
David, Thy forefather, in a Psalm names Thee the ark of divine holiness, O
Most pure one, who didst contain in a manner surpassing nature, God seated in the bosom of the Father, Whom without ceasing we the faithful magnify.

9.refrain: Most holy Theotokos, save us.
9.text: Thou art truly higher than all creation, O Maiden; since for our sake thou hast given birth in the flesh to the Creator of all things; therefore, as Mother of the only Master, thou hast majestically brought about victory for us against all adversaries.

hymns.text: O most compassionate Master and God, by the judgments which Thou knowest grant that I may have the fear of Thee in my heart, that I may spurn the works of the evil one, love Thee with all my soul, and do Thy saving will; for Thou art our God, Who said: Ask, and ye shall receive.
hymns.verse: If Thou shouldest mark iniquities, О Lord, О Lord, who shall stand? * For with Thee there is forgiveness.

hymns.text: I have become a mockery for the demons and contemptible for men, lamentation for the righteous and weeping for the angels, polluting the air, the earth and the waters; for I have defiled my body and sullied my soul and mind with countless evil acts, and have become an enemy to God. Woe is me, О Lord! I have sinned, I have sinned against Thee! Forgive me!
hymns.verse: For Thy name’s sake have I patiently waited for Thee, О Lord; my soul hath waited patiently for Thy word, * my soul hath hoped in the Lord.

hymns.text: I pray Thee: Be Thou patient with me, who am devoid of fruits, О Master, and cut me not down like the barren tree with the axe of death, dispatching me to the fire; but be Thou entreated to make me fruitful, giving me time for repentance, in that Thou lovest mankind, that I may wash away my many sins, О Christ my Savior.
hymns.verse: From the morning watch until night, from the morning watch * let Israel hope in the Lord.

hymns.text: О Forerunner, who didst live a blameless life in the desert, restore thou my mind, which hath become barren through sins.
hymns.verse: For with the Lord there is mercy, and with Him there is plenteous redemption; * and He shall redeem Israel out of all his iniquities.

hymns.text: We glorify thee, О prophet, understanding thee to be the swallow heralding the divine spring to those in the world.
hymns.verse: О praise the Lord, all ye nations; * praise Him, all ye peoples.

hymns.text: О divine Forerunner, intercessor for my life, protector of my soul: Deliver me, thy servant, from the false accusation of man.
hymns.verse: For He hath made His mercy to prevail over us, * and the truth of the Lord abideth forever.

glory.text: O Lady, to the right tranquil haven of salvation guide me who am tempesttossed amid the tumult of slothfulness.

theotokion.text: O Lady, to the right tranquil haven of salvation guide me who am tempesttossed amid the tumult of slothfulness.

prokeimenon.refrain: The Lord will hearken unto me * when I cry unto Him.
prokeimenon.verse: When I called upon Thee, O God of my righteousness, Thou didst

hymns.text: I have come, О Compassionate One, like the prodigal son. As one of Thy hirelings do Thou accept me who fall down before Thee, О God, and have mercy on me, О Lover of mankind.

Like the one who fell among thieves and was wounded, so have I fallen through many sins, and my soul hath been wounded. To whom shall I who am guilty flee? If not to Thee alone, the Physician of our souls. О God, pour forth upon me Thy great mercy!
hymns.verse: Unto Thee have I lifted up mine eyes, unto Thee that dwellest in heaven. Behold, as the eyes of servants look unto the hands of their masters, as the eyes of the handmaid look unto the hands of her Mistress, so do our eyes look unto the Lord our God, * until He take pity on us.

hymns.text: О holy martyrs, ye who have fought the good fight and received crowns: Entreat the Lord, that He have mercy upon our souls.
hymns.verse: Have mercy on us, О Lord, have mercy on us, for greatly are we filled with abasement. Greatly hath our soul been filled therewith; let reproach come upon them that prosper, * and abasement on the proud.

glory.text: Rejoice, O Sovereign Lady, thou cloud of the noetic and ineffable Sun! * Rejoice, all-luminous lantern: Rejoice all-golden candle-stand. * Through thee, O most holy one, * hath Eve been delivered from the curse. * But as thou dost possess boldness before thy Son and God * Who is readily moved to compassion, * fail not to entreat Him by thy maternal supplication, ** O most pure one.

theotokion.text: Rejoice, O Sovereign Lady, thou cloud of the noetic and ineffable Sun! * Rejoice, all-luminous lantern: Rejoice all-golden candle-stand. * Through thee, O most holy one, * hath Eve been delivered from the curse. * But as thou dost possess boldness before thy Son and God * Who is readily moved to compassion, * fail not to entreat Him by thy maternal supplication, ** O most pure one.

hymns.text: Of old, a tree drove me from paradise, but now a tree hath restored me to paradise when Thou wast crucified, О Christ.
hymns.verse: If Thou shouldest mark iniquities, О Lord, О Lord, who shall stand? * For with Thee there is forgiveness.

hymns.text: О awesome wonder! How can the Creator, standing before a created being, be condemned and crucified for the salvation of mankind?
hymns.verse: For Thy name’s sake have I patiently waited for Thee, О Lord; my soul hath waited patiently for Thy word, * my soul hath hoped in the Lord.

hymns.text: О Cross of Christ, the amazement of the holy angels and the great wounding of the demons: Save thy servants!
hymns.verse: From the morning watch until night, from the morning watch * let Israel hope in the Lord.

hymns.text: Beholding her Lamb stretched out upon the Tree, the Virgin, the unblemished ewe-lamb, cried aloud: “Woe is me, О my most sweet Child! What is this strange and most glorious mystery? How hath the iniquitous assembly lifted Thee up upon a Cross, and given Thee gall to drink, Who fed me with manna?”
hymns.verse: For with the Lord there is mercy, and with Him there is plenteous redemption; * and He shall redeem Israel out of all his iniquities.

hymns.text: When Thy Virgin Mother beheld Thee nailed by the Jews to the Cross on Golgotha, condemned by an unrighteous verdict, О my Christ, she cried out: “Woe is me, О my most beloved Son! What is this strange sight? How can the senseless children of the Jews nail Thee, the Lord of all, to the Cross?”
hymns.verse: О praise the Lord, all ye nations; * praise Him, all ye peoples.

hymns.text: О all-holy Lady, we have all come to know thee alone as her who was manifest as a virgin even after giving birth. And when thou beheldest Him to Whom thou hast given birth, with His hands willingly nailed to the Tree, thou didst cry out: “O Longsuffering One, Thou dost willingly die, delivering all who hymn Thee from death!”
hymns.verse: For He hath made His mercy to prevail over us, * and the truth of the Lord abideth forever.

glory.text: О all-holy Virgin Lady, who hast given birth unto the Lord Who in His tender compassion was nailed to the Cross, pouring forth a stream of life upon the world: Entreat Him, that our souls may be saved; for thee alone do we the faithful have as a refuge, bulwark and aid. Wherefore, we flee to thy protection.

theotokion.text: О all-holy Virgin Lady, who hast given birth unto the Lord Who in His tender compassion was nailed to the Cross, pouring forth a stream of life upon the world: Entreat Him, that our souls may be saved; for thee alone do we the faithful have as a refuge, bulwark and aid. Wherefore, we flee to thy protection.

prokeimenon.refrain: Thy mercy, O Lord, shall pursue me * all the days of my life.
prokeimenon.verse: The Lord is my shepherd, and I shall not want. In a place of green

hymns.text: No longer are we forbidden the Tree of life, for we have Thy Cross as our hope. О Lord, glory be to Thee!
Suspended upon the Tree, О Immortal One, Thou didst break the snares of the devil. О Lord, glory be to Thee!
hymns.verse: Unto Thee have I lifted up mine eyes, unto Thee that dwellest in heaven. Behold, as the eyes of servants look unto the hands of their masters, as the eyes of the handmaid look unto the hands of her Mistress, so do our eyes look unto the Lord our God, * until He take pity on us.

hymns.text: Caring naught for all the things of earth, О holy martyrs, and having manfully preached Christ at the tribunal, ye received from Him rewards for your torments; but as ye have boldness, beseech Him, as the almighty God, that He save the souls of us who flee to you, we pray.
hymns.verse: Have mercy on us, О Lord, have mercy on us, for greatly are we filled with abasement. Greatly hath our soul been filled therewith; let reproach come upon them that prosper, * and abasement on the proud.

glory.text: Once thou didst behold thy son upon the Tree, * thy heart was pierced by a lance of sadness, ** O most-pure One.

theotokion.text: Once thou didst behold thy son upon the Tree, * thy heart was pierced by a lance of sadness, ** O most-pure One.

hymns.text: Having tilled the whole earth with the plough of the knowledge of God, О glorious apostles, ye caused a multitude of the faithful to spring forth.
hymns.verse: If Thou shouldest mark iniquities, О Lord, О Lord, who shall stand? * For with Thee there is forgiveness.

hymns.text: Set at naught the winter of my passions, О blessed apostles, and shine forth upon me the pure spring of peace.
hymns.verse: For Thy name’s sake have I patiently waited for Thee, О Lord; my soul hath waited patiently for Thy word, * my soul hath hoped in the Lord.

hymns.text: As disciples of the Word ye brought the assemblies of the nations over from senselessness to the knowledge of God.
hymns.verse: From the morning watch until night, from the morning watch * let Israel hope in the Lord.

hymns.text: As of old thou didst By thy prayers deliver the officers who were to be executed, О father Nicholas, so do thou now save us.
hymns.verse: For with the Lord there is mercy, and with Him there is plenteous redemption; * and He shall redeem Israel out of all his iniquities.

hymns.text: О holy and most blessed hierarch Nicholas, deliver from griefs all who invoke and hymn thee with faith and love.
hymns.verse: О praise the Lord, all ye nations; * praise Him, all ye peoples.

hymns.text: Free thy servants from famine and pestilence, from earthquake and tribulations, and from all want, О most wise and holy hierarch Nicholas.
hymns.verse: For He hath made His mercy to prevail over us, * and the truth of the Lord abideth forever.

glory.text: Convey the entreaty of thy servants to thy Son, О most pure one, that He may save all whom He hath created.

theotokion.text: Convey the entreaty of thy servants to thy Son, О most pure one, that He may save all whom He hath created.

prokeimenon.refrain: O God, in Thy name save me, * and in Thy strength do
prokeimenon.verse: O God, hearken unto my prayer, give ear unto the words of my

hymns.text: О glorious apostles, pillars of the Church, preachers of the Truth, radiant beacons: With the fire of the Spirit ye consumed all delusion and illumined the race of mankind with faith. Wherefore, we beseech you: Entreat our Savior and God, that He grant peace to the world and save our souls.

О apostles of Christ, husbandmen of the Savior, bearing the Cross upon your shoulders as a plough, and having cleared the earth made hard by the delusion of idolatry, ye sowed the word of faith. And ye are fittingly honored, О holy apostles of Christ.
hymns.verse: Unto Thee have I lifted up mine eyes, unto Thee that dwellest in heaven. Behold, as the eyes of servants look unto the hands of their masters, as the eyes of the handmaid look unto the hands of her Mistress, so do our eyes look unto the Lord our God, * until He take pity on us.

hymns.text: О all-praised martyrs, spiritual lambs, reason-endowed holocausts, acceptable sacrifices well-pleasing to God: the earth could not hide you, but heaven received you, and ye have become communicants with the angels. With them entreat our Savior and God, we pray you, that He grant peace to the world and save our souls.
hymns.verse: Have mercy on us, О Lord, have mercy on us, for greatly are we filled with abasement. Greatly hath our soul been filled therewith; let reproach come upon them that prosper, * and abasement on the proud.

glory.text: Let us hymn as the Mother of God the Word, her who alone was an all-pure virgin after giving birth, saying: Glory be to thee!

theotokion.text: Let us hymn as the Mother of God the Word, her who alone was an all-pure virgin after giving birth, saying: Glory be to thee!

hymns.text: That Thou mightest make man a god, Thou didst become a man and wast crucified, О supremely good Christ. Glory be to Thy power!
hymns.verse: If Thou shouldest mark iniquities, О Lord, О Lord, who shall stand? * For with Thee there is forgiveness.

hymns.text: When the assembly of the Jews condemned Thee to die on the Cross, О Jesus, the earth quaked and the sun hid its light.
hymns.verse: For Thy name’s sake have I patiently waited for Thee, О Lord; my soul hath waited patiently for Thy word, * my soul hath hoped in the Lord.

hymns.text: The iniquitous assembly crowned Thee with thorns, О Immortal and holy King, Who cuttest off the thorns of delusion at the root.
hymns.verse: From the morning watch until night, from the morning watch * let Israel hope in the Lord.

hymns.text: Beholding her Lamb stretched out upon the Tree, the Virgin, the unblemished ewe-lamb, cried aloud: “Woe is me, О my most sweet Child! What is this strange and most glorious mystery? How hath the iniquitous assembly lifted Thee up upon a Cross, and given Thee gall to drink, Who fed me with manna?”
hymns.verse: For with the Lord there is mercy, and with Him there is plenteous redemption; * and He shall redeem Israel out of all his iniquities.

hymns.text: When Thy Virgin Mother beheld Thee nailed by the Jews to the Cross on Golgotha, condemned by an unrighteous verdict, О my Christ, she cried out: “Woe is me, О my most beloved Son! What is this strange sight? How can the senseless children of the Jews nail Thee, the Lord of all, to the Cross?”
hymns.verse: О praise the Lord, all ye nations; * praise Him, all ye peoples.

hymns.text: О all-holy Lady, we have all come to know thee alone as the one who wast revealed to be a virgin after giving birth. And when thou didst behold Him to Whom thou hast given birth, with His hands willingly nailed to the Tree, thou didst cry out: “O Longsuffering One, Thou dost willingly die, delivering all who hymn Thee from death!”
hymns.verse: For He hath made His mercy to prevail over us, * and the truth of the Lord abideth forever.

glory.text: О all-holy Virgin Lady, who hast given birth unto the Lord Who in His loving-kindness was nailed to the Cross, pouring forth a stream of life upon the world: Entreat Him, that our souls may be saved; for thee alone do we, the faithful have as a refuge, bulwark and aid. Wherefore, we flee to thy protection.

theotokion.text: О all-holy Virgin Lady, who hast given birth unto the Lord Who in His loving-kindness was nailed to the Cross, pouring forth a stream of life upon the world: Entreat Him, that our souls may be saved; for thee alone do we, the faithful have as a refuge, bulwark and aid. Wherefore, we flee to thy protection.

prokeimenon.refrain: My help cometh from the Lord, * Who hath made heaven
prokeimenon.verse: I have lifted up mine eyes to the Mountains, from whence cometh

hymns.text: No longer are we forbidden the Tree of life, for we have Thy Cross as our hope. О Lord, glory be to Thee!
Suspended upon the Tree, О Immortal One, Thou didst break the snares of the devil. O Lord, glory be to Thee!
hymns.verse: Unto Thee have I lifted up mine eyes, unto Thee that dwellest in heaven. Behold, as the eyes of servants look unto the hands of their masters, as the eyes of the handmaid look unto the hands of her Mistress, so do our eyes look unto the Lord our God, * until He take pity on us.

hymns.text: Glory be to Thee, О Christ God, Thou boast of the apostles and joy of the martyrs, who preached the consubstantial Trinity!
hymns.verse: Have mercy on us, О Lord, have mercy on us, for greatly are we filled with abasement. Greatly hath our soul been filled therewith; let reproach come upon them that prosper, * and abasement on the proud.

glory.text: When the all-immaculate one beheld Thee willingly nailed to the Tree, weeping, she hymned Thy dominion.

theotokion.text: When the all-immaculate one beheld Thee willingly nailed to the Tree, weeping, she hymned Thy dominion.

hymns.text: The martyrs dispelled the darkness of ungodliness, showing forth the light of divine knowledge unto all mankind.
hymns.verse: If Thou shouldest mark iniquities, О Lord, О Lord, who shall stand? * For with Thee there is forgiveness.

hymns.text: With Orthodox teachings as with divine rays ye enlightened the Church of the Lord, О most wise pastors.
hymns.verse: For Thy name’s sake have I patiently waited for Thee, О Lord; my soul hath waited patiently for Thy word, * my soul hath hoped in the Lord.

hymns.text: Ever dwelling in trackless wastes, ye broke the nets of the demons, О venerable fathers.
hymns.verse: From the morning watch until night, from the morning watch * let Israel hope in the Lord.

hymns.text: О Savior, when Thou comest to judge the whole world put me not to shame, for I have committed shameful acts.
hymns.verse: For with the Lord there is mercy, and with Him there is plenteous redemption; * and He shall redeem Israel out of all his iniquities.

hymns.text: Glory be to Thee, O Christ God, Thou boast of the apostles, joy of the martyrs; whose preaching was of the consubstantial Trinity.
hymns.verse: О praise the Lord, all ye nations; * praise Him, all ye peoples.

hymns.text: O holy martyrs, ye who have fought the good fight and received your crowns, pray to the Lord, that our souls be saved.
hymns.verse: For He hath made His mercy to prevail over us, * and the truth of the Lord abideth forever.

glory.text: Thou hast been known to have become a Mother * in a manner surpassing nature O Theotokos, * and hast remained a Virgin in a manner beyond all telling and understanding; * no tongue can expound the wonder of thy birthgiving. * For while thy conceiving O Pure one, was most glorious, * the manner of thy birthgiving transcends comprehension; * for where God so willeth, the order of nature is overthrown. * Wherefore, we all, knowing thee to be the Mother of God, * do earnestly entreat thee: ** Pray thou that our souls be saved!

theotokion.text: Thou hast been known to have become a Mother * in a manner surpassing nature O Theotokos, * and hast remained a Virgin in a manner beyond all telling and understanding; * no tongue can expound the wonder of thy birthgiving. * For while thy conceiving O Pure one, was most glorious, * the manner of thy birthgiving transcends comprehension; * for where God so willeth, the order of nature is overthrown. * Wherefore, we all, knowing thee to be the Mother of God, * do earnestly entreat thee: ** Pray thou that our souls be saved!

prokeimenon.refrain: O God, my helper art Thou, * and Thy mercy shall go
prokeimenon.verse: Rescue me from mine enemies, O God, and from them that rise up

hymns.text: Despising every earthly thing, O holy martyrs, and having bravely preached Christ at the tribunal, ye received your reward from Him for your torments. Since ye have boldness before God, we beseech you to pray to Him as one almighty, that He save the souls of us who flee unto you.

O all-praised martyrs, spiritual lambs, reason endowed whole burnt offerings and sacrifices acceptable and well-pleasing to God. Ye were not hidden by the earth, but heaven hath received you; and ye have become companions of the angels we entreat you to pray with them unto our God and Savior, that He grant peace to the world, and save our souls.
hymns.verse: Blessed are they whom Thou hast chosen * and taken to Thyself, О Lord.

hymns.text: For the departed О Thou Who in the beginning fashioned man in Thine image and in accordance with Thy likeness, in paradise Thou didst appoint him to rule over Thy creatures; but, led astray by the malice of the devil, he partook of the fruit, breaking Thy commandment. Wherefore, Thou didst condemn him to return to the earth from whence he had been taken, О Lord, and to beg for repose.
hymns.verse: Their souls * shall dwell among good things.

hymns.text: О our Savior, grant rest to our brethren whom Thou hast removed from transitory things, and who cry: Glory be to Thee, О Lord.

glory.text: О thou who alone didst receive the uncontainable Word of God, and hast given birth to Him incarnate: Pray that our souls be saved.

theotokion.text: О thou who alone didst receive the uncontainable Word of God, and hast given birth to Him incarnate: Pray that our souls be saved.

dogmatikon.label: Theotokion — Dogmatikon
dogmatikon.text:
The King of heaven, because of His love for man,
appeared on earth and dwelt with men.
He took flesh from the pure Virgin
and after assuming it, He came forth from her.
The Son is one: in two natures, yet one Person.
Proclaiming Him as perfect God and perfect Man,
we confess Christ our God!
Entreat Him, O unwedded Mother, to have mercy on our souls.

resurrectional.note: Resurrectional stichera from OCA Obikhod (TT). Used at Saturday Great Vespers, Lord I Have Cried, verses 6–1.

hymns.text:
We offer to Thee, O Christ, our evening song and reasonable service, for Thou didst will to have mercy on us
by Thy Resurrection.

Lord, Lord, do not cast us away from Thy face! For Thou didst will to have mercy on us
by Thy Resurrection.

Rejoice, O holy Zion: Mother of the churches, the abode of God, for thou wast the first to receive remission of sins
by the Resurrection.

The Word of God the Father, begotten before the ages, in the latter times willed to be incarnate of the Virgin and endured crucifixion unto death. He has saved mortal man
by His Resurrection.

We glorify Thee, O Lord, for Thou didst voluntarily endure the Cross for our sake. We fall down in worship before Thee, O almighty Savior. Do not cast us away from Thy face but hear us and save us, O Lover of man,
by Thy Resurrection!

Glory to Thee, O Savior Christ, Onlybegotten Son of God, who wast nailed to the Cross
and resurrected from the tomb on the third day!

glory.label: Resurrectional Doxastichon
glory.text:
We glorify Thy Resurrection from the dead, O Christ, by which Thou hast freed Adam's race from the tortures of hell,
and as God hast granted to the world eternal life and great mercy.

hymns.label: Idiomelon
hymns.text:
Descending from heaven to ascend the Cross, the eternal Life has come for death to raise those who are fallen; to enlighten those in darkness.
O Jesus, our Savior and Illuminator, glory to Thee!

hymns.label: Sticheron 2
hymns.text:
We glorify Christ, Who rose from the dead. He accepted the human body and soul, and freed both from suffering. His most pure soul descended to hell, which He spoiled. His holy body did not see corruption in the tomb.
He is the Redeemer of our souls.

hymns.label: Sticheron 3
hymns.text:
In psalms and songs we glorify Thy Resurrection from the dead, O Christ. By it, Thou hast freed us from the tortures of hell
and as God hast granted eternal life and great mercy.

hymns.label: Sticheron 4
hymns.text:
O Master of all, incomprehensible Creator of heaven and earth, by Thy suffering on the Cross Thou hast gained passionlessness for me. Accepting burial and arising in glory, Thou didst resurrect Adam with Thyself by Thine almighty hand. Glory to Thy rising on the third day! By it Thou hast granted us eternal life and cleansing of sins
as the only compassionate One.

hymns.label: Sticheron 5
hymns.text:
O Master of all, incomprehensible Creator of heaven and earth, by Thy suffering on the Cross Thou hast gained passionlessness for me. Accepting burial and arising in glory, Thou didst resurrect Adam with Thyself by Thine almighty hand. Glory to Thy rising on the third day! By it Thou hast granted us eternal life and cleansing of sins
as the only compassionate One.

theotokion.label: Aposticha Theotokion
theotokion.text:
O unwedded Virgin, who ineffably didst conceive God in the flesh, O Mother of God most high, accept the cries of thy servants, O blameless one! Grant cleansing of transgressions to all!
Receive our prayers and pray to save our souls!

troparion.label: Resurrectional Troparion
troparion.text:
Thou didst descend from on high, O Merciful One! Thou didst accept the threeday burial to free us from our sufferings!
O Lord, our Life and Resurrection, glory to Thee!

kontakion.label: Resurrectional Kontakion
kontakion.text: By rising from the tomb, Thou didst raise the dead and resurrect Adam. Eve exults in Thy Resurrection, and the world celebrates Thy rising from the dead, O greatly Merciful One!

dismissalTheotokion.label: Resurrectional Dismissal Theotokion
dismissalTheotokion.text: For our sake Thou wast born of the Virgin and didst endure crucifixion, O Good One, destroying death by death. Revealing the Resurrection as God, do not despise the work of Thy hand. Reveal Thy love for man, O Merciful One, and accept the Theotokos praying for us, and save the despairing people, O our Savior.

hymns.text: О Lord Who camest into the world to call sinners to Thee, and Who accepted the thief, the publican and the harlot: In Thy love for mankind, О my Christ, call me also to Thee, though I have sinned against Thee more than all others, and never repent.
hymns.verse: If Thou shouldest mark iniquities, О Lord, О Lord, who shall stand? * For with Thee there is forgiveness.

hymns.text: О Lord, Who ordained publicans as pastors for Thy Church, and made a helper now of him who before was a persecutor: By their supplications show me to be Thy lamb, О Savior, and let me not, who am useless, fall prey to the alien one.
hymns.verse: For Thy name’s sake have I patiently waited for Thee, О Lord; my soul hath waited patiently for Thy word, * my soul hath hoped in the Lord.

hymns.text: Weep before the end, О my wretched and most vile soul, and cleave unto God, crying out to him with groans from the depths of thy heart: I have sinned against Thee, О Christ! Revile me not, I pray, but turning me back to Thee, grant me forgiveness, in that Thou art merciful.
hymns.verse: From the morning watch until night, from the morning watch * let Israel hope in the Lord.

hymns.text: О Lord, the councils of the incorporeal beings unceasingly glorify Thee, delighting in Thy beauty and ineffable comeliness, richly illumined by the light of the threefold Sun. By their prayers and intercession, О Savior, save Thou our souls.
hymns.verse: For with the Lord there is mercy, and with Him there is plenteous redemption; * and He shall redeem Israel out of all his iniquities.

hymns.text: О Lord, by Thine omnipotent word and Thy most holy Spirit Thou didst arrange the harmonious choirs of the angels, imparting effulgence to them with never-waning rays. By their prayers and intercession, О Savior, save Thou our souls.
hymns.verse: О praise the Lord, all ye nations; * praise Him, all ye peoples.

hymns.text: О Lord, the seraphim hymn Thee, and the authorities, angels, and principalities, the dominions, thrones and powers, the archangels and the dread cherubim offer Thee praise. By their prayers and intercession, О Savior, save Thou our souls.
hymns.verse: For He hath made His mercy to prevail over us, * and the truth of the Lord abideth forever.

glory.text: I, a created being, ever offend the Creator and move Him to anger. Grant me correction, О Maiden, first conciliating Him; and with thy help raise me up to pleasing works, that I may receive salvation and remission.

theotokion.text: I, a created being, ever offend the Creator and move Him to anger. Grant me correction, О Maiden, first conciliating Him; and with thy help raise me up to pleasing works, that I may receive salvation and remission.

prokeimenon.refrain: Behold now, bless ye the Lord, * all ye servants of the Lord.
prokeimenon.verse: Ye that stand in the house of the Lord, in the courts of the house of

hymns.text: The angels unceasingly hymn Thee, the King and Master; and I fall down before Thee, crying like the publican: Cleanse me, О God, and have mercy upon me!
As thou art immortal, О my soul, let not the waves of life cover thee, but rise up, crying out to thy Benefactor: Cleanse me, О God, and save me!
hymns.verse: Unto Thee have I lifted up mine eyes, unto Thee that dwellest in heaven. Behold, as the eyes of servants look unto the hands of their masters, as the eyes of the handmaid look unto the hands of her Mistress, so do our eyes look unto the Lord our God, * until He take pity on us.

hymns.text: О martyrs of the Lord, ye sanctify every place and heal every infirmity. Pray ye now, that our souls be delivered from the snares of the enemy, we beseech you.
hymns.verse: Have mercy on us, О Lord, have mercy on us, for greatly are we filled with abasement. Greatly hath our soul been filled therewith; let reproach come upon them that prosper, * and abasement on the proud.

glory.text: Taking up the cry of the Archangel Gabriel, let us say: * Rejoice, O Mother of God, * who hast given birth unto Christ, ** the bestower of life upon the world!

theotokion.text: Taking up the cry of the Archangel Gabriel, let us say: * Rejoice, O Mother of God, * who hast given birth unto Christ, ** the bestower of life upon the world!

beatitudes.note: Canon of the Resurrection, Odes 3 and 6. Standard Sunday "On 8" assignment.

ode3.irmos: O Christ fortify me on the rock of Thy commandments, Thou who in the beginning didst establish the heavens with understanding and didst establish the earth upon the waters, for there is none holy save Thee, O only Lover of mankind.
troparia: The salvific Passion of Thy flesh, O Christ, hath justified Adam, who had been condemned by the taste of sin; for Thou, who alone art without sin, hast revealed that Thou didst remain uncondemned by the trial of death.

O Jesus my God, Thou hast made the light of the Resurrection to shine forth upon those that sit in the darkness and shadow of death, and by Thy divinity Thou hast bound the strong one and scattered his spoils.
ode3.theotokion: Thou wast revealed to be higher than the Cherubim and Seraphim, O Theotokos, for thou alone hast received within thy womb, O undefiled one, God who is uncircumscribable; and so with hymns we believers ever call thee blessed.

ode6.irmos: Cleanse me, O Savior, for many are mine iniquities; lead me up from the abyss of evils I pray Thee, for unto Thee have I cried, and Thou hast hearkened unto me, O God of my salvation.
troparia: Through a tree the author of evil hath mightily overthrown me, but raised upon a Cross, O Christ, Thou didst more mightily cast him down, confounding him, whilst raising me the one who had fallen.
When Thou didst shine forth from the grave, then didst Thou take pity on Zion, and in Thy compassion didst renew it by Thy divine blood, and now O Christ, Thou dost reign over it as King forever.
ode6.theotokion: May we be delivered from grievous faults by thine intercessions, O pure Birthgiver of God, and may we receive the divine radiance of the Son of God, who ineffably became incarnate from thee.

afterKathisma2.text:
Thou the Life of all, didst rise from the dead, and an Angel of light cried out to the women saying: “Cease your tears. Bring the good tidings unto the
Apostles.” Cry aloud in hymns that Christ the Lord hath arisen who as God was well-pleased to save mankind.

When Thou wast indeed risen from the tomb Thou didst command the holy women to announce the Resurrection to the Apostles, as it is written; and
Peter, having arrived quickly, stood by the grave and seeing the light in the tomb was affrightened. observing the grave clothes lying therein, without the divine body, and believing he cried aloud: “Glory be to Thee O Christ God our
Savior, Who hast saved us all, for Thou art the effulgence of the Father.”

afterKathisma2.label: Glory…, Now…, Theotokion
afterKathisma2.text: Let us hymn the heavenly gate and ark, the all-holy mountain, the cloud of light, the heavenly ladder, the spiritual Paradise, the redemption of Eve, the great treasure of the world; because salvation for the world and forgiveness of ancient offences were wrought in her. Therefore we cry unto her: Intercede with thine own Son and God to grant forgiveness offences to those who devoutly worship thy most holy Offspring.

afterKathisma3.text: Mortals have sealed Thy tomb, O Savior and an Angel hath rolled away the stone from the door. Women saw Thee risen from the dead, and announced the good tidings to Thy Disciples in Zion: “The life of all hath risen and the bonds of death are loosed. O Lord, glory be to Thee.”

The women having brought sweet-smelling burial spices heard an Angel’s voice coming from the tomb: “Cease thy tears, and bring joy instead of sorrow.” Wherefore Cry ye aloud in hymns that Christ the Lord is risen, who as God was well-pleased to save the race of mankind.

afterKathisma3.label: Glory…, Now…, Theotokion
afterKathisma3.text:
In Thee, O Full of grace, doth all creation rejoice, the ranks of Angels and the race of mankind; O all-hallowed Temple and spiritual Paradise, boast of
Virgins. For from thee God became incarnate and He who is our God before the ages became a child. He hath made thy womb a throne and rendered it wider than the heavens. In thee, O Full of grace, doth all creation rejoice; glory be to thee.

matins.hypakoe: The myrrh-bearing women standing at the tomb of the Giver of life seeking the immortal Master among the dead; and having received the glad tidings of joy from the Angel announced unto the Apostles that Christ the Lord is risen, granting the world great mercy.

troparia:
From my youth the enemy doth tempt me, enflaming me with the desire for pleasures; but placing my trust in Thee O Lord, I put him to flight.
Let those that hate Zion, become like grass before it is tilled; for Christ severeth their necks with the sharp blade of torments.
By the Holy Spirit all things have life; Light from Light, eminent God: we hymn Him together with the Father and the Word.

Let my humble heart be sheltered by the fear of Thee; lest it fall away from
Thee by being conceited, O exceedingly compassionate One.
He who hath his hope in the Lord will not be afraid when the Lord judgeth all things with fire and torment.

Everyone inspired by the Holy Spirit seeth and foretelleth all, working the greatest wonders, singing of one God in three Hypostases; for though the
Divinity radiates with triune light, it ruleth as one.

I have cried unto Thee, O Lord, hearken unto me, bend Thine ear to my supplications when I cry unto Thee, and do thou cleanse me before taking me from this life.
Each and every one who returneth to mother earth will depart to receive torments or rewards in reward for their life’s actions.

Contemplation of God by the Holy Spirit is of a thrice-holy unity; for the
Father is beginningless, from Whom the Son was begotten before time, and the Spirit equal in essence and majesty, doth blaze forth equally from the
Father.

Behold, what is so good, what is so pleasant as to see brothers dwelling together? For by this the Lord hath promised eternal life.
The One who adorneth the lilies of the field doth command us to be unconcerned over temporal things.
By the Holy Spirit, by one single cause all things gain the reward of peace; for He is God perfectly consubstantial with both the Father and the Son.

prokeimenon.refrain: The Lord shall be king unto eternity; thy God, O Zion, unto generation and generation.
prokeimenon.verse: Praise the Lord, O my soul. I will praise the Lord in my life.

matins.postGospelSticheron: Jesus having risen from the grave, as He foretold, hath given us life eternal, and great mercy.

laudsStichera.verse: To do among them the judgment that is written This glory shall be to all His saints.
laudsStichera.text:
O Lord, though Thou didst stand before the judgment seat being judged by
Pilate, yet Thou wast seated with the Father and was not absent from Thy throne. And risen from the dead Thou hast freed the world from slavery to the stranger, as Thou art full of compassionate pity and love for mankind.

laudsStichera.verse: Praise ye God in His saints, praise Him in the firmament of His power.
laudsStichera.text: O Lord, the Jews laid Thee as a corpse in a grave, and soldiers guarded Thee as a sleeping king sealing Thee with a seal as if a treasury of life; but Thou didst arise and grant incorruption to our souls.

laudsStichera.verse: Praise Him for His mighty acts, praise Him according to the multitude of His greatness.
laudsStichera.text: O Lord, Thou hast given us Thy Cross as a weapon against the devil; for he doth quake and tremble, unable to contemplate Thy power; for Thou didst raise the dead and abolish death: Wherefore we worship Thy Burial and Thy Rising.

laudsStichera.verse: Praise Him with the sound of trumpet, praise Him with the psaltery and harp.
laudsStichera.text: Thine Angel, O Lord, having proclaimed Thy Resurrection, filled the guards with fear, but he cried unto the women saying: “Why seek ye the living among the dead? Being God He is risen granting life to the whole world.”

laudsStichera.verse: Praise Him with timbrel and dance, praise him with strings and flute.
laudsStichera.text:
Thou didst endure the Cross, O Thou who art impassible in Thy Divinity, to free us from slavery to the enemy and Thou didst accept burial for three days, making us immortal, and granting life unto us through Thy Resurrection, O
Christ God, Lover of mankind.

laudsStichera.verse: Praise Him with tuneful cymbals, praise Him with cymbals of jubilation. Let every breath praise the Lord.
laudsStichera.text: O Christ, I worship, I glorify, and I praise Thy Resurrection from the tomb, through which Thou hast delivered us from the unbreakable bonds of Hades and as God hast granted the world eternal life and great mercy.

laudsStichera.verse: Arise, O Lord my God, let Thy hands be lifted high; forget not Thy paupers to the end.
laudsStichera.text: The Lawless men, watching over Thy grave which had received life, sealed it; but Thou, as the immortal and all-powerful God, arose on the third day.

laudsStichera.verse: I will confess Thee, O Lord, with my whole heart, I will tell of all Thy wonders.
laudsStichera.text: When Thou didst enter the gates of Hades, O Lord, and didst shatter them, the prisoners therein cried aloud: “Who is this?, for He hath not been condemned to the lowest parts of the earth, but hath torn down the prisons of death as if a tent, we received Him as One mortal, and we tremble before Him as God.” O All-powerful Savior have mercy on us.

canonIrmoi.1: The wonderworking staff of Moses, striking and dividing the sea in the figure of a cross, once drowned Pharaoh the pursuing charioteer, while it saved the fleeing people of Israel as they fled on foot, chanting a hymn unto God.
canonIrmoi.3:
O Christ fortify me on the rock of Thy commandments, Thou who in the beginning didst establish the heavens with understanding and didst establish the earth upon the waters, for there is none holy save
Thee, O only Lover of mankind.
canonIrmoi.4:
Thou, O Lord, art my strength and Thou art my power, Thou art my God and Thou art my joy, Thou Who, while never leaving the bosom of Thy Father, hast visited our poverty. Therefore with the Prophet
Habbakuk I cry unto Thee, ‘Glory to Thy power, O Lover of mankind!’
canonIrmoi.5: O Light never-waning, why hast Thou turned Thy face from me and why hath the alien darkness surrounded me, wretched though I be? But do Thou guide my steps I implore Thee and turn me back towards the light of Thy commandments.
canonIrmoi.6: Cleanse me, O Savior, for many are mine iniquities; lead me up from the abyss of evils I pray Thee, for unto Thee have I cried, and Thou hast hearkened unto me, O God of my salvation.
canonIrmoi.7:
Once in Babylon the fire stood in awe of God's condescension; for which sake the youths in the furnace, dancing with joyous steps as in a meadow, chanted: O God of our fathers,
Blessed art Thou!
canonIrmoi.8:
In his wrath the Chaldean Tyrant made the furnace blaze, with heat fanned sevenfold for the servants of God; but when he perceived that they had been saved by a greater power he cried aloud to the Creator and
Redeemer; ‘ye children bless, ye priests praise, ye people, supremely exalt Him throughout all ages’.
canonIrmoi.9: Heaven was stricken with awe, and the ends of the earth were filled with amazement, for God hath appeared in the flesh, and thy womb was rendered more spacious than the heavens. Wherefore, the ranks of men and of angels magnify thee as the Theotokos.

1.refrain: Glory to Thy holy Resurrection, O Lord.
1.text: How can we not but marvel at Christ’s all-powerful divinity? To the faithful it poureth forth dispassion from his passion, while from His holy side, it sheddeth forth a fount of incorruption, and from His tomb, eternal life.

1.refrain: Glory to Thy holy Resurrection, O Lord.
1.text: How glorious the Angel doth now appear to the Women, wearing the luminous attributes of natural immaterial purity; for by his countenance he revealeth the radiance of the Resurrection as he crieth aloud, “The Lord hath been raised!”

1.refrain: Glory to Thy holy Resurrection, O Lord.
1.text: Glorious things have been spoken of thee in generation after generation, O Virgin Theotokos, who, while remaining pure, didst contain within thy womb God the Word. Wherefore, after God, we all honor thee as our protection.

1.refrain: Glory to Thy precious Cross and Resurrection, O Lord.
1.text: The gates of the Hades of suffering have been destroyed, and its gatekeepers tremble in fear upon seeing in the lowest depths the One who on high supranaturally surpasseth the nature of all things.

1.refrain: Glory to Thy precious Cross and Resurrection, O Lord.
1.text:
The ranks of Angels stood amazed when they saw mankind’s fallen nature, which had been held fast in the lowest depths, now seated upon the throne of the
Father.

1.refrain: Glory to Thy precious Cross and Resurrection, O Lord.
1.text: O Mother without bridegroom, the ranks of Angels and of mortal mankind sing thy praise without ceasing, for thou didst carry their Creator as an infant in thine arms.

1.refrain: Most holy Theotokos, save us.
1.text: Immaculate Mother of God, who hast given birth beyond nature to the incarnate and eternal Word, we sing thy praises.

1.refrain: Most holy Theotokos, save us.
1.text: The Virgin hath given birth unto Thee, O Christ, the cluster of grapes from whence drippeth the life-bearing sweetness of the world’s salvation.

1.refrain: Most holy Theotokos, save us.
1.text: The race of Adam, having now been raised to blessedness beyond all telling, doth fittingly glorify thee, O Theotokos.

3.refrain: Glory to Thy holy Resurrection, O Lord.
3.text: The salvific Passion of Thy flesh, O Christ, hath justified Adam, who had been condemned by the taste of sin; for Thou, who alone art without sin, hast revealed that Thou didst remain uncondemned by the trial of death.

3.refrain: Glory to Thy holy Resurrection, O Lord.
3.text:
O Jesus my God, Thou hast made the light of the Resurrection to shine forth upon those that sit in the darkness and shadow of death, and by Thy divinity
Thou hast bound the strong one and scattered his spoils.

3.refrain: Glory to Thy holy Resurrection, O Lord.
3.text:
Thou wast revealed to be higher than the Cherubim and
Seraphim, O Theotokos, for thou alone hast received within thy womb, O undefiled one, God who is uncircumscribable; and so with hymns we believers ever call thee blessed.

3.refrain: Glory to Thy precious Cross and Resurrection, O Lord.
3.text: When of old I disobeyed Thy commandments, O Lord Who hath fashioned me, Thou didst reckon me a stranger; however having refashioned me and taught me obedience, Thou hast reconciled me to Thyself through the Crucifixion.

3.refrain: Glory to Thy precious Cross and Resurrection, O Lord.
3.text: Having made thine abode within a Virgin, Thou O Lord, didst appear in the flesh to mankind, as befitted Thee to be seen. And Thou didst reveal her to be truly the Mother of God and the succor of believers, O only Lover of mankind.

3.refrain: Most holy Theotokos, save us.
3.text: Grant help unto me by thine intercessions, O all-pure one, by warding off the assaults of dreaded dangers.

3.refrain: Most holy Theotokos, save us.
3.text: When thou, O Theotokos, didst give birth to the prince of life on behalf of all the world, Thou didst become the restoration of our foremother Eve.

3.refrain: Most holy Theotokos, save us.
3.text: By thy power grant me life, O all-pure one, who hath truly given birth to God in the flesh, the hypostatic power of the Father.

4.refrain: Glory to Thy holy Resurrection, O Lord.
4.text: While I was hostile towards Thee, Thou didst love me exceedingly, for by a wondrous self-emptying, Thou didst descend to earth, O compassionate Savior, not spurning the indignity of the coarseness of my state, yet remaining in the height of Thine ineffable glory, whereby Thou hast glorified me who had hitherto existed in dishonor.

4.refrain: Glory to Thy holy Resurrection, O Lord.
4.text: Who now doth not stand in awe, O Master, upon seeing death destroyed through Thy suffering, corruption taking flight through Thy Cross, and Hades emptied of its wealth through Thy death? These actions result from Thy lofty power, O Thou Crucified Lover of mankind.

4.refrain: Glory to Thy holy Resurrection, O Lord.
4.text: Thou art the boast of the faithful, O Mother unwedded, thou art the protection, thou art the refuge of Christians, their wall and safe harbor; for thou dost bring their supplications before thy Son, O all-immaculate One, and savest from dangers those who with faith and love acknowledge thee to be the pure Theotokos.

4.refrain: Glory to Thy precious Cross and Resurrection, O Lord.
4.text:
The lawless and disobedient children nailed Thee to the Cross, O Lover of mankind, but in Thy compassion Thou hast, through it, saved those who glorify
Thy sufferings.

4.refrain: Glory to Thy precious Cross and Resurrection, O Lord.
4.text:
By arising from the grave, Thou hast raised with Thee all the dead in Hades, and in Thy loving compassion Thou hast enlightened those who glorify Thy
Resurrection.

4.refrain: Glory to Thy precious Cross and Resurrection, O Lord.
4.text: O Immaculate Mary, implore God whom thou didst bare to grant thy supplicants forgiveness of their offences.

4.refrain: Most holy Theotokos, save us.
4.text: O Theotokos, thou art the un-ploughed land that brought forth the ear of corn which granteth life unto the world, do thou save those who sing thy praises.

4.refrain: Most holy Theotokos, save us.
4.text:
All we who have been enlightened know thee, O all-pure one, to be the
Mother of God, for thou, O Ever-Virgin, didst bear the Sun of righteousness.

4.refrain: Most holy Theotokos, save us.
4.text:
Grant us the pardon of our offences, as thou alone art without sin, and grant peace unto thy world, O God, by the supplications of her who hath given birth to
Thee.

5.refrain: Glory to Thy holy Resurrection, O Lord.
5.text:
O Savior, Thou didst endure being wrapped in a cloak as Thou wast mocked before Thy Passion, thus covering the unsightly nakedness of the first-formed
Adam, and being nailed to the Cross naked, Thou didst strip from Thyself, O
Christ, the tunic of death.

5.refrain: Glory to Thy holy Resurrection, O Lord.
5.text: Rising out of the dust of death, O Christ, Thou hast refashioned my fallen nature and rendered it incorrupt, revealing it as once again a princely image, radiating with the light of incorruption.

5.refrain: Glory to Thy holy Resurrection, O Lord.
5.text:
Having obtained a mother’s freedom of speech before thy Son,
O all-pure one, we beseech thee to neglect not thy maternal care for us, for thee alone do we Christians present to the Master as a compassionate means of atonement.

5.refrain: Glory to Thy precious Cross and Resurrection, O Lord.
5.text: Guide us and grant us peace by the power of Thy Cross, O Christ, for by it we fall down before Thee, O Lover of mankind.

5.refrain: Glory to Thy precious Cross and Resurrection, O Lord.
5.text: O our God, guide the lives of us who sing the praises of Thine arising, and grant us peace, O only Lover of mankind.

5.refrain: Glory to Thy precious Cross and Resurrection, O Lord.
5.text: O Mary, most pure and revered, who knew not wedlock, implore thy Son and our God to send down upon us the faithful, His great mercy.

5.refrain: Most holy Theotokos, save us.
5.text:
Calm the stormy tempest of my passions, O thou who hast given birth to
God, my guide and my Lord.

5.refrain: Most holy Theotokos, save us.
5.text:
The ranks of Angels and the companies of mortals worship thine Offspring, O
Immaculate Theotokos.

5.refrain: Most holy Theotokos, save us.
5.text: O Mary Theotokos thou who without bridegroom hast brought to naught the expectations of our enemies, bring joy to those who hymn thy praises.

6.refrain: Glory to Thy holy Resurrection, O Lord.
6.text: Through a tree the author of evil hath mightily overthrown me, but raised upon a Cross, O Christ, Thou didst more mightily cast him down, confounding him, whilst raising me the one who had fallen.

6.refrain: Glory to Thy holy Resurrection, O Lord.
6.text:
When Thou didst shine forth from the grave, then didst Thou take pity on
Zion, and in Thy compassion didst renew it by Thy divine blood, and now O
Christ, Thou dost reign over it as King forever.

6.refrain: Glory to Thy holy Resurrection, O Lord.
6.text:
May we be delivered from grievous faults by thine intercessions,
O pure Birthgiver of God, and may we receive the divine radiance of the Son of
God, who ineffably became incarnate from thee.

6.refrain: Glory to Thy precious Cross and Resurrection, O Lord.
6.text:
Thou didst stretch forth Thy hands upon the Cross, thus healing the hand of
Adam the first-formed, which he so greedily stretched forth in Eden, and instead of the bitter tree, O Christ, Thou didst taste gall, and as all-powerful, Thou dost save those who glorify Thy sufferings.

6.refrain: Glory to Thy precious Cross and Resurrection, O Lord.
6.text: The Redeemer tasted of the ancient sentence of death that He might abolish the palace of corruption, and when He had visited those in Hades, He saved, as one all-powerful, those who hymn the praises of His Resurrection.

6.refrain: Glory to Thy precious Cross and Resurrection, O Lord.
