6.text: Cease not to intercede for us, O Most holy Virgin Theotokos, support of the faithful, for by our trust in thee we are made strong, therefore with love we glorify thee and Him who from thee ineffably assumed flesh.

6.refrain: Most holy Theotokos, save us.
6.text: O Theotokos, we the faithful proclaim thee to be the Temple and Ark, the living Bridal Chamber of God, and the gate of heaven.

6.refrain: Most holy Theotokos, save us.
6.text: Mary, bride of God, thine Offspring, Who hath become the destroyer of wooden idols, is worshipped together with the Father and the Spirit.

6.refrain: Most holy Theotokos, save us.
6.text: The Word of God revealed thee to mortal mankind to be a heavenly ladder, for through thee He descended to us.

7.refrain: Glory to Thy holy Resurrection, O Lord.
7.text:
Thy glorious self-emptying, constituting the divine wealth of Thy poverty, O
Christ, amazed the Angels when they saw Thee nailed to the Cross, saving those who with faith cry aloud, “O God of our fathers, Blessed art Thou!”

7.refrain: Glory to Thy holy Resurrection, O Lord.
7.text:
Upon Thy divine descent the regions beneath the earth were filled with light, and the darkness which previously pursued those therein, was driven out.
Therefore the prisoners from every age arose, crying aloud, “O God of our fathers, Blessed art Thou!”

7.refrain: Glory to Thy holy Resurrection, O Lord.
7.text:
Trinitarian: Speaking of God with Orthodox belief, we proclaim Thee O Lord of all, to be Father of the one only-begotten Son, and we know only one right
Spirit Who proceedeth from Thee, consubstantial and co-eternal.

7.refrain: Glory to Thy precious Cross and Resurrection, O Lord.
7.text: Thou hast wrought salvation in the midst of the inhabited world, O God, as the Prophet said, for lifted up upon the Tree, Thou hast called back all those who cry out to Thee with faith, “O God of our fathers, Blessed art Thou!”

7.refrain: Glory to Thy precious Cross and Resurrection, O Lord.
7.text:
Rising from the tomb as from sleep, O compassionate Lord, Thou hast raised the world with Thee, while creation, through the Apostles’ preaching of Thine
Arising, hath been persuaded to cry to Thee. “Blessed are Thou O God of our
Fathers!”

7.refrain: Glory to Thy precious Cross and Resurrection, O Lord.
7.text:
Equal in action, and equal in power and co-eternal with His
Begetter, the Word is fashioned in the womb of the Virgin by the good pleasure of the Father through the activity of the Spirit. “Blessed are Thou O God of our
Fathers!”

7.refrain: Most holy Theotokos, save us.
7.text: For our salvation Thou didst appear incarnate from a virginal womb, and knowing Thy Mother to be the one who gave birth to God, we cry with thanksgiving, “Blessed are Thou O God of our Fathers!”

7.refrain: Most holy Theotokos, save us.
7.text: O Virgin, thou art the most blessed rod from Jesse’s root, blossoming with a salvific fruit for those who with faith cry to thy Son, “Blessed are Thou O God of our Fathers!”

7.refrain: Most holy Theotokos, save us.
7.text:
O Hypostatic Wisdom of the Most High, through the Theotokos fill with wisdom and divine power all those who sing to Thee in faith, “Blessed are Thou
O God of our Fathers!”

8.refrain: Glory to Thy holy Resurrection, O Lord.
8.text: In a manner befitting God, the supreme divine power of Jesus’ Divinity hath shone forth in our nature: for having tasted of death in the flesh upon the Cross for the sake of all, He hath abolished the strength of Hades. Without ceasing: O ye Youths bless, O ye priests praise, O ye people supremely exalt Him throughout all ages.

8.refrain: Glory to Thy holy Resurrection, O Lord.
8.text: The Crucified One hath arisen, the boastful One hath fallen, the fallen and crushed have been set upright, corruption hath been banished and incorruption hath blossomed forth; for mortality hath been swallowed up by life. O ye Youths bless, O ye priests praise, O ye people supremely exalt Him throughout all ages.

8.refrain: Glory to Thy holy Resurrection, O Lord.
8.text: Trinitarian: The Godhead of triune light, the single radiance shining forth from one three-hypostatic nature, the Begetter without beginning, the Word of the Father, one with him in Nature and Kingship, and the consubstantial Spirit; O ye Youths bless, O ye priests praise, O ye people supremely exalt Him throughout all ages.

8.refrain: Glory to Thy precious Cross and Resurrection, O Lord.
8.text:
Unto the Lord who, stripped naked, hath stretched out His hands upon the
Tree for me, unto Him who doth call me and warm me with his noble nakedness, do all ye works of the Lord bless, and supremely exalt throughout the ages.

8.refrain: Glory to Thy precious Cross and Resurrection, O Lord.
8.text: The Lord who hath raised me the fallen one, out of the lowest pits of Hades, and honored me with the high-throned glory of his Begetter, do all ye works of the Lord, bless, and supremely exalt throughout the ages.

8.refrain: Glory to Thy precious Cross and Resurrection, O Lord.
8.text:
Thou didst appear as a daughter of fallen Adam, but also as the
Mother of the God who hath renewed my nature. Therefore all we His works sing
His praises as Lord and supremely exalt throughout the ages.

8.refrain: Most holy Theotokos, save us.
8.text: Do thou quench the seductive and fiery darts of those who are our enemies, that we may hymn thee, O pure Maiden, throughout the ages.

8.refrain: Most holy Theotokos, save us.
8.text:
In a manner surpassing nature, O Virgin, thou hast given birth to God the
Word, the Creator and Savior; therefore we hymn thee throughout the ages.

8.refrain: Most holy Theotokos, save us.
8.text: The unapproachable Light, Who made His abode within thee, O Virgin, hath shown thee to be a radiant golden lamp throughout the ages.

9.refrain: Glory to Thy holy Resurrection, O Lord.
9.text:
Begotten simply in Thy divine nature which is without beginning, Thou didst become compound in nature by assuming flesh, giving it essence in Thyself, O
Word of God, and enduring the passion as a man, Thou didst remain beyond suffering as God, wherefore we magnify Thee in two natures inseparable and uncommingled.

9.refrain: Glory to Thy holy Resurrection, O Lord.
9.text:
In accordance with Thy divine nature, O Most High, Thou didst address God as Thy Father when Thou didst descend to Thy servants and become man; and having risen from the dead Thou didst make Him who is by nature God and
Master, Father by grace, of those born of earth, wherefore we magnify Thee together with Him.

9.refrain: Glory to Thy holy Resurrection, O Lord.
9.text:
When thou didst give birth in the body in a manner surpassing nature to the good Word, Who came forth from the Father’s own essence before all ages, as He alone is Good, thou O Virgin was revealed as the Mother of God.
Him we now comprehend to be beyond the nature of flesh, even though He is clothed in a natural body.

9.refrain: Glory to Thy precious Cross and Resurrection, O Lord.
9.text:
We know that Thou art the Son of God by nature, conceived in the womb of the Theotokos, and that Thou didst become man for our sake, and, as we behold
Thee hanging upon the Cross, we understand that Thou didst suffer in Thy human nature, yet as God Thou didst remain untouched by suffering.

9.refrain: Glory to Thy precious Cross and Resurrection, O Lord.
9.text: Murky darkness hath been abolished, for from Hades Christ, the Sun of righteousness, hath dawned enlightening all the ends of the earth, radiant with the rays of his Divinity, man from heaven, God on earth, whom we magnify in two natures.

9.refrain: Glory to Thy precious Cross and Resurrection, O Lord.
9.text:
Bend Thy bow, prosper and reign, O Son of the Theotokos, subdue the people of Ishmael who war against us, and grant unto all Orthodox
Christians the Cross as an invincible weapon, and trophy of peace.

9.refrain: Most holy Theotokos, save us.
9.text: We are filled with joy and gladness at the thought of thee, overflowing with healing for those who approach thee and devoutly proclaim thee to be the Mother of God.

9.refrain: Most holy Theotokos, save us.
9.text: With psalms we sing thy praises, O thou who art Full of grace, and never silent, we ceaselessly offer thee our praises; for thou art a fount of rejoicing for all.

9.refrain: Most holy Theotokos, save us.
9.text: Fair is thy fruit, O Theotokos, for those who partake of it, it is incorruption, and for those who magnify thee with faith, it is life.

hymns.text: I have acquired neither compunction, * nor a wellspring of tears, * nor fervent confession, * nor weeping which washeth me clean, nor humility of heart; * I have been neither an emulator of the publican, * nor of the harlot, nor of the prodigal son. * How, therefore, shall I find remission for my many sins? * But in the judgments which Thou knowest, ** save me, О Christ.
hymns.verse: If Thou shouldest mark iniquities, О Lord, О Lord, who shall stand? * For with Thee there is forgiveness.

hymns.text: I have made myself a stranger * to every commandment of God; * in every way I have neglected higher virtue; * mindlessly wasting my whole life in slothfulness; * and I have committed * every unseemly and iniquitous act * in fornication. * Wherefore, since Thou art compassionate О Christ, ** have pity, and freely save me.
hymns.verse: For Thy name’s sake have I patiently waited for Thee, О Lord; my soul hath waited patiently for Thy word, * my soul hath hoped in the Lord.

hymns.text: In Thine anger rebuke me not * who am the work of Thy hands, * and who with my foolish mind * have torn myself away from goodness, О Lover of mankind, * and Who in the abyss of Thine ineffable compassion * wast for my sake well-pleased to become like unto me. * But through the supplications of Thine Ever-virgin Mother, О Word, * grant me divine conversion, ** in that Thou art God.
hymns.verse: From the morning watch until night, from the morning watch * let Israel hope in the Lord.

hymns.text: О blessed Forerunner John, * with love ever return my lowly soul * to the Lord, * and by thy sacred mediations * quench for me the fire of pleasures, * guiding me to the fulfillment * of the precepts of God, * and truly cleansing the senses of my heart, ** that I may glorify thee.
hymns.verse: For with the Lord there is mercy, and with Him there is plenteous redemption; * and He shall redeem Israel out of all his iniquities.

hymns.text: О all-praised Forerunner John, * thou offspring of a barren woman, * pure orchard of the Master * and adornment of mankind, * divine preserver of my lowly soul: * By thy prayers and aid * grant me readiness to forgive, * delivering me from the wiles of the serpent ** and his wicked treachery and attacks.
hymns.verse: О praise the Lord, all ye nations; * praise Him, all ye peoples.

hymns.text: Wholly delightful and full of divine bliss, * О ever-glorious one, * thou hast gladdened all * who come to thee with faith, * delighting the senses of our souls and bodies, * ever releasing us from infirmities * and tribulations, * from the assaults of evil, ** and soul-corrupting pleasures.
hymns.verse: For He hath made His mercy to prevail over us, * and the truth of the Lord abideth forever.

glory.text: With faith I hasten to thy protection, * О pure Birthgiver of God. * Save me from perils and misfortunes, * from the confusion of the passions and the malice of the demons. * For, possessed of an abyss of mercy, * thou hast been shown to be a mediatress of salvation, * О Lady who hast given birth to the only merciful, * all-compassionate and abundantly kind-hearted God.

theotokion.text: With faith I hasten to thy protection, * О pure Birthgiver of God. * Save me from perils and misfortunes, * from the confusion of the passions and the malice of the demons. * For, possessed of an abyss of mercy, * thou hast been shown to be a mediatress of salvation, * О Lady who hast given birth to the only merciful, * all-compassionate and abundantly kind-hearted God.

prokeimenon.refrain: The Lord will hearken unto me * when I cry unto Him.
prokeimenon.verse: When I called upon Thee, O God of my righteousness, Thou didst

hymns.text: The angels unceasingly hymn Thee, the King and Master; and I fall down before Thee, crying like the publican: Cleanse me, О God, and have mercy upon me!
As thou art immortal, О my soul, let not the waves of life engulf thee, but rise up, crying out to thy Benefactor: Cleanse me, О God, and save me!
hymns.verse: Unto Thee have I lifted up mine eyes, unto Thee that dwellest in heaven. Behold, as the eyes of servants look unto the hands of their masters, as the eyes of the handmaid look unto the hands of her Mistress, so do our eyes look unto the Lord our God, * until He take pity on us.

hymns.text: О martyrs of the Lord, entreat ye our God, and ask for our souls a multitude of compassions and the cleansing of our many transgressions, we beseech you.
hymns.verse: Have mercy on us, О Lord, have mercy on us, for greatly are we filled with abasement. Greatly hath our soul been filled therewith; let reproach come upon them that prosper, * and abasement on the proud.

glory.text: Rejoice, thou praise of the universe! * Rejoice, temple of the Lord! * Rejoice, mountain overshadowed! * Rejoice, refuge of all! * Rejoice, golden candlestick! * Rejoice, honored glory of the Orthodox! * Rejoice, Mary, Mother of Christ God! * Rejoice, paradise! Rejoice, divine table! * Rejoice, tabernacle! Rejoice, golden jar! ** Rejoice, thou hope of all!

theotokion.text: Rejoice, thou praise of the universe! * Rejoice, temple of the Lord! * Rejoice, mountain overshadowed! * Rejoice, refuge of all! * Rejoice, golden candlestick! * Rejoice, honored glory of the Orthodox! * Rejoice, Mary, Mother of Christ God! * Rejoice, paradise! Rejoice, divine table! * Rejoice, tabernacle! Rejoice, golden jar! ** Rejoice, thou hope of all!

hymns.text: When Thou wast nailed to the Cross, Thy hands and feet run through, Thy holy side was pierced, pouring forth drops of blood and water, our divine salvation, O Supremely good One, that Thou mightest wash away my defilement and pollution. Glory to Thy goodness, О all-Compassionate One!
hymns.verse: If Thou shouldest mark iniquities, О Lord, О Lord, who shall stand? * For with Thee there is forgiveness.

hymns.text: Thou didst endure suffering, О Master, that Thou mightest bestow dispassion upon all who worship Thy sufferings and voluntary sacrifice: the spear, nails and reed, which Thou didst willingly endure with long-suffering: that for the sake of Thy sufferings, О Lord, Thou mightest win dispassion for me.
hymns.verse: For Thy name’s sake have I patiently waited for Thee, О Lord; my soul hath waited patiently for Thy word, * my soul hath hoped in the Lord.

hymns.text: The unblemished heifer, beholding her Bullock willingly lifted up upon the Tree, cried out with compunction, lamenting: “Woe is me, О my most beloved Child! How hath the ungrateful assembly of the Jews rewarded Thee, desiring to leave me bereft of Thee, О all-Beloved!”
hymns.verse: From the morning watch until night, from the morning watch * let Israel hope in the Lord.

hymns.text: “I cannot bear to see Thee Who givest wakefulness to all, asleep upon the Tree, that Thou mightest give divine and saving watchfulness to those who have fallen into pernicious sleep through the fruit of disobedience!”, said the Virgin, whom we magnify, weeping.
hymns.verse: For with the Lord there is mercy, and with Him there is plenteous redemption; * and He shall redeem Israel out of all his iniquities.

hymns.text: “The unrighteous council lifted Thee the Lamb Who taketh away the sins of the world up upon the Cross, and they pierced Thy side with a spear, and ran Thy hands and feet through with nails, О Long- suffering One. О the wicked savagery! О the audacity!”, cried the most pure one, weeping with compunction.
hymns.verse: О praise the Lord, all ye nations; * praise Him, all ye peoples.

hymns.text: “What is this thing that I now behold, О Master? Thou Who most wondrously holdeth all creation in the palm of Thy hand art suspended unjustly upon the Tree as a lamb, О Word of God, hung there by disobedient servants. О the patience! О Thy goodness, О Compassionate One!”, the most immaculate one said, weeping.
hymns.verse: For He hath made His mercy to prevail over us, * and the truth of the Lord abideth forever.

glory.text: When the most pure one beheld Thee led to the slaughter, * following Thee with tears she cried aloud: * Whither hast Thou gone, O my Son,? * I can no longer bear not beholding Thee ** O my abundantly merciful Jesus.'

theotokion.text: When the most pure one beheld Thee led to the slaughter, * following Thee with tears she cried aloud: * Whither hast Thou gone, O my Son,? * I can no longer bear not beholding Thee ** O my abundantly merciful Jesus.'

prokeimenon.refrain: Thy mercy, O Lord, shall pursue me * all the days of my life.
prokeimenon.verse: The Lord is my shepherd, and I shall not want. In a place of green

hymns.text: О Christ God Who wast lifted up upon the Cross, Thou didst save the race of mankind. We glorify Thy sufferings!
Thou wast nailed to the Cross, О Christ God, and opened the gates of paradise. We glorify Thy divinity!
hymns.verse: Unto Thee have I lifted up mine eyes, unto Thee that dwellest in heaven. Behold, as the eyes of servants look unto the hands of their masters, as the eyes of the handmaid look unto the hands of her Mistress, so do our eyes look unto the Lord our God, * until He take pity on us.

hymns.text: Thy martyrs, О Lord, putting aside the things of life, ignored their tortures for the sake of the life which is to come, and were shown to be inheritors thereof; wherefore, they rejoice with the angels. By their supplications grant great mercy to Thy people.
hymns.verse: Have mercy on us, О Lord, have mercy on us, for greatly are we filled with abasement. Greatly hath our soul been filled therewith; let reproach come upon them, that prosper, * and abasement on the proud.

glory.text: O Lord, when the sun beheld Thee * the Sun of righteousness, hanging upon the Tree, * it hid its rays, and the light of the moon was changed to darkness; * and Thine all-immaculate Mother ** was pierced in the depths of her soul.

theotokion.text: O Lord, when the sun beheld Thee * the Sun of righteousness, hanging upon the Tree, * it hid its rays, and the light of the moon was changed to darkness; * and Thine all-immaculate Mother ** was pierced in the depths of her soul.

hymns.text: О Lord, Thou didst enlighten Thine apostles with the beams of the Comforter, with the noetic radiance of the knowledge of Thee setting them as beacons unto the confirmation of the Faith, О Master; wherefore, we bow down before Thine ineffable love for mankind.
hymns.verse: If Thou shouldest mark iniquities, О Lord, О Lord, who shall stand? * For with Thee there is forgiveness.

hymns.text: By the supplications of Thine apostles, О Lord, Thou hast protected this Thy flock, preserving it unharmed by the temptations of the enemy; for with Thy precious blood Thou didst redeem it from enslavement to the enemy, in that Thou art compassionate and the Lover of mankind.
hymns.verse: For Thy name’s sake have I patiently waited for Thee, О Lord; my soul hath waited patiently for Thy word, * my soul hath hoped in the Lord.

hymns.text: Together ye were shown to be like precious stones set in the foundation of the Church, with radiant brilliance shining forth upon the whole world the knowledge of God, О divine apostles, who stand before the Trinity and pray for our souls.
hymns.verse: From the morning watch until night, from the morning watch * let Israel hope in the Lord.

hymns.text: О Lord, Thou hast glorified thine honored and holy hierarch Nicholas to the ends of the earth, imparting to him the grace of miracles; and Thou didst show him to be the champion of those in grievous misfortunes, and those who have fallen into tribulations and afflictions, and who ever ask his help.
hymns.verse: For with the Lord there is mercy, and with Him there is plenteous redemption; * and He shall redeem Israel out of all his iniquities.

hymns.text: О Lord, have pity on Thy servants through the divine entreaties of Thy holy hierarch, and free us from all misfortune, all affliction, and every attack, for with faith do we bow down before Thine invincible might.
hymns.verse: О praise the Lord, all ye nations; * praise Him, all ye peoples.

hymns.text: Having thee as an intercessor and fervent ally before the Master, О Nicholas, we flee to thee with faith, and cry: Turn us not away empty of thy protection, but show that thy loving-kindness extends to thy servants.
hymns.verse: For He hath made His mercy to prevail over us, * and the truth of the Lord abideth forever.

glory.text: Behold the groaning of my contrite heart, * O Bride of God, * and accept, and reject not, the lifting up of my hands, * O pure and all-immaculate Virgin Mary, * as thou art full of love for mankind; ** that I may hymn and magnify thee who hast exalted our race.

theotokion.text: Behold the groaning of my contrite heart, * O Bride of God, * and accept, and reject not, the lifting up of my hands, * O pure and all-immaculate Virgin Mary, * as thou art full of love for mankind; ** that I may hymn and magnify thee who hast exalted our race.

prokeimenon.refrain: O God, in Thy name save me, * and in Thy strength do
prokeimenon.verse: O God, hearken unto my prayer, give ear unto the words of my

hymns.text: Fervently loving Thee on earth, О Lord, Thine apostles considered all to be but dung, that they might acquire Thee alone; and they gave their bodies over to wounds for Thee; wherefore, glorified, they pray for our souls.

О Lord, Thou didst magnify the memory of the apostles on earth, for assembling them together, we all glorify Thee; since by their prayers and for their sake, Thou dost grant healings, peace and great mercy to the whole world.
hymns.verse: Unto Thee have I lifted up mine eyes, unto Thee that dwellest in heaven, Behold, as the eyes of servants look unto the hands of their masters, as the eyes of the handmaid look unto the hands of her Mistress, so do our eyes look unto the Lord our God, * until He take pity on us.

hymns.text: What virtue, what praise is due the saints? For they bowed their heads beneath the sword for the sake of Thee Who bowed down the heavens and descended; they shed their blood for Thee Who emptied Thyself and assumed the form of a servant; they humbled themselves even unto death, emulating Thy poverty. By their prayers, О God, have mercy upon us in the multitude of Thy compassions.
hymns.verse: Have mercy on us, О Lord, have mercy on us, for greatly are we filled with abasement. Greatly hath our soul been filled therewith; let reproach come upon them that prosper, * and abasement on the proud.

glory.text: I flee to thy protection, O holy Virgin Theotokos, * for I know that through thee I shall obtain salvation; ** for thou art able to help me, O pure one.

theotokion.text: I flee to thy protection, O holy Virgin Theotokos, * for I know that through thee I shall obtain salvation; ** for thou art able to help me, O pure one.

hymns.text: О most glorious wonder! * The Life-bearing Tree, * the most holy Cross is revealed today, * lifted up on high. * All the ends of the earth glorify it, * and the hordes of the demons are affrighted. * О what a gift hath been given to mortals! * Thereby, О Christ, save Thou our souls, ** in that Thou alone art compassionate.
hymns.verse: If Thou shouldest mark iniquities, О Lord, О Lord, who shall stand? * For with Thee there is forgiveness.

hymns.text: О most glorious wonder! * Like a vine full of life, * bearing the Most High, * the Cross is seen today uplifted from the earth. * Thereby have we all been drawn to God, * and death hath been utterly slaughtered. * О most honored Tree, * whereby, glorifying Christ, * we have received the immortal sustenance ** which was in Eden!
hymns.verse: For Thy name’s sake have I patiently waited for Thee, О Lord; my soul hath waited patiently for Thy word, * my soul hath hoped in the Lord.

hymns.text: О the great goodness which Thou hast for us, * О good Jesus! * How didst Thou abase Thyself, * become a man, and will to suffer, * enduring the Cross and violent death * for Thy useless servants? * We offer the Cross to Thee as a worthy and divinely fitting gift; * and giving thanks, ** we, the faithful, glorify Thee.
hymns.verse: From the morning watch until night, from the morning watch * let Israel hope in the Lord.

hymns.text: О the ineffable goodness of Him * Who became incarnate from thee, О allhymned Theotokos! * For He Who is the Lover of mankind * endured the Cross and death, * that He might save what He created. * Entreat Him, that He may deliver me from torment, * the wretched one who am greatly sick, * and cause me to dwell ** where the never-waning light shines.
hymns.verse: For with the Lord there is mercy, and with Him there is plenteous redemption; * and He shall redeem Israel out of all his iniquities.

hymns.text: “What is this sight which mine eyes behold, * О Master? * Lifted up upon the Tree, * Thou Who upholdest all creation dost die, * giving life unto all!”, * said the Theotokos, weeping, * when she saw the God and man * who ineffably shone forth from her ** suspended upon the Cross.
hymns.verse: О praise the Lord, all ye nations; * praise Him, all ye peoples.

hymns.text: О most pure one, * by thine honored supplications * deliver from misfortunes the flock which thy Son and God * sprinkled with His blood. * And fending off the invisible foe-like wolves, * fill their faces with shame, * as David the psalmist * proclaimed of old, ** О pure one.
hymns.verse: For He hath made His mercy to prevail over us, * and the truth of the Lord abideth forever.

glory.text: Beholding Thee nailed to the Cross * and voluntarily accepting suffering, O Jesus, * Thy Virgin Mother, O Master, * cried aloud: Woe is me, my sweet Child! * How is it that Thou dost endure * unjustly inflicted wounds, * O Physician Who healeth the infirmities of mankind, * delivering all from corruption ** in Thy tender compassion?

theotokion.text: Beholding Thee nailed to the Cross * and voluntarily accepting suffering, O Jesus, * Thy Virgin Mother, O Master, * cried aloud: Woe is me, my sweet Child! * How is it that Thou dost endure * unjustly inflicted wounds, * O Physician Who healeth the infirmities of mankind, * delivering all from corruption ** in Thy tender compassion?

prokeimenon.refrain: My help cometh from the Lord, * Who hath made heaven
prokeimenon.verse: I have lifted up mine eyes to the Mountains, from whence cometh

hymns.text: О Christ God Who wast lifted up upon the Cross, Thou didst save the race of mankind. We glorify Thy sufferings!
Thou wast nailed to the Cross, О Christ God, and didst open the gates of paradise. We glorify Thy divinity!
hymns.verse: Unto Thee have I lifted up mine eyes, unto Thee that dwellest in heaven. Behold, as the eyes of servants look unto the hands of their masters, as the eyes of the handmaid look unto the hands of her Mistress, so do our eyes look unto the Lord our God, * until He take pity on us.

hymns.text: Ye sanctify everyplace, О martyrs of the Lord, and heal every infirmity. Pray ye now, that our souls be delivered from the nets of the enemy, we beseech you.
hymns.verse: Have mercy on us, О Lord, have mercy on us, for greatly are we filled with abasement. Greatly hath our soul been filled therewith; let reproach come upon them that prosper, * and abasement on the proud.

glory.text: The unblemished heifer, beholding her Bullock * willingly nailed upon the Tree, * cried out aloud, lamenting piteously: * “Woe is me, O my most beloved Child! * How hath the ungrateful assembly of the Jews rewarded Thee, * desiring to leave me childless and bereft of Thee, ** O my most beloved Child?”

theotokion.text: The unblemished heifer, beholding her Bullock * willingly nailed upon the Tree, * cried out aloud, lamenting piteously: * “Woe is me, O my most beloved Child! * How hath the ungrateful assembly of the Jews rewarded Thee, * desiring to leave me childless and bereft of Thee, ** O my most beloved Child?”

hymns.text: O martyrs of the Lord, ye sanctify every place and heal every manner of infirmities: and now we entreat you to intercede on our behalf, that our souls may be delivered from the snares of the enemy.
hymns.verse: If Thou shouldest mark iniquities, О Lord, О Lord, who shall stand? * For with Thee there is forgiveness.

hymns.text: Thy martyrs, O Lord, no longer mindful of the temporal things of this life, despised not torture for the sake of the life to come, which they manifestly inherited, wherefore they rejoice with the angels. By their supplications grant great mercy to Thy people.
hymns.verse: For Thy name’s sake have I patiently waited for Thee, О Lord; my soul hath waited patiently for Thy word, * my soul hath hoped in the Lord.

hymns.text: What virtue, what praise, are due the saints? For they bowed their heads beneath the sword for the sake of Thee Who bowed down the heavens and descended to us; they shed their blood for Thee Who abased Thyself and assumed the form of a servant; they humbled themselves even unto death, imitating Thy poverty. By their intercessions, О God, have mercy on us, in the multitude of Thy compassions!
hymns.verse: From the morning watch until night, from the morning watch * let Israel hope in the Lord.

hymns.text: I have become like the barren tree, as if clad with useless leaves; and I am afraid that if I am cut down, Thou wilt send me into unquenchable everlasting fire, О Master. But grant me time to convert, that I may offer Thee the goodly fruit of virtuous acts, and may be deemed worthy of Thy kingdom.
hymns.verse: For with the Lord there is mercy, and with Him there is plenteous redemption; * and He shall redeem Israel out of all his iniquities.

hymns.text: O Lord, О Lord, Who lovest mankind, turn not Thy face away from me, Thy servant, who angereth Thy goodness every day, neither punish me by Thy righteous wrath, О Christ. I confess that I have sinned, I have sinned against Thee like none other. But have pity and save me, by the prayers of Thy Mother.
hymns.verse: О praise the Lord, all ye nations; * praise Him, all ye peoples.

hymns.text: When in Thy glory Thou shalt sit as the King of all upon Thy judgment-seat, and all the holy angels stand before Thee with fear, and all human nature will stand before Thee to be judged, О Christ; then, through the supplications of Thy Mother, О Lord, from all torments deliver those who have fallen asleep in faith.
hymns.verse: For He hath made His mercy to prevail over us, * and the truth of the Lord abideth forever.

glory.text: In His love for mankind, the King of heaven appeared on earth * and dwelt among men; * for He Who received flesh from the pure Virgin * and cameth forth from her having received human nature, * is the only Son of God, * twofold in nature but not Hypostasis. * Therefore, proclaiming Him to be truly perfect God and perfect man, * we confess Christ our God. * Him do thou beseech, O unwedded Mother, ** that our souls find mercy!

theotokion.text: In His love for mankind, the King of heaven appeared on earth * and dwelt among men; * for He Who received flesh from the pure Virgin * and cameth forth from her having received human nature, * is the only Son of God, * twofold in nature but not Hypostasis. * Therefore, proclaiming Him to be truly perfect God and perfect man, * we confess Christ our God. * Him do thou beseech, O unwedded Mother, ** that our souls find mercy!

prokeimenon.refrain: O God, my helper art Thou, * and Thy mercy shall go
prokeimenon.verse: Rescue me from mine enemies, O God, and from them that rise up

hymns.text: O ye martyrs of the Lord, we beseech you to intercede before our God: pray ye that abundant mercy be granted to our souls, and the cleansing of our many sins.

I weep and lament when I contemplate death and behold our beauty, which hath been created according to the image of God, lying in the grave, bereft of form, devoid of glory, unsightly. О the wonder! What is this mystery concerning us? How have we been given over to corruption? How have we been yoked together with death? Truly, as it is written, this is by the command of God, Who giveth rest unto the departed.
hymns.verse: Blessed are they whom Thou hast chosen * and taken to Thyself, О Lord.

hymns.text: Thy death, О Lord, won immortality for us; for if Thou hadst not been laid in the tomb, paradise would not have been opened. Wherefore, grant rest to the departed, in that Thou lovest mankind.
hymns.verse: Their souls * shall dwell among good things.

glory.text: O pure Virgin, portal of the Word, Mother of our God: pray thou that we be saved.

theotokion.text: O pure Virgin, portal of the Word, Mother of our God: pray thou that we be saved.
